entries.name: Dothan Houston County Library System — Library of Things
entries.description: One of the state's deepest collections (~50+ items): guitars, turntables, power tools, pressure washers, sewing machines, heat press, cameras, gaming consoles, kitchen gear. Free 1-week checkout (age 19+) from the Main Library front desk; also runs a seed library.
entries.address: Main Library, 445 N Oates St, Dothan, AL 36303

entries.name: Homewood Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends tools, musical instruments, electronics, outdoor items, record-player kits (with a vinyl collection), Tonies, games, STEM and health-and-wellness kits — a genuinely broad "things" collection for a suburban library.
entries.hours: not listed on LoT page (see site)

entries.name: Mobile Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Tech-leaning collection: Chromebooks, Wi-Fi hotspot kits, Playaway Launchpads and audiobooks, educational "CLAP" toys, and video games. Loans 7–21 days depending on item.
entries.address: Ben May Main Library, 701 Government St, Mobile, AL 36602

entries.name: North Shelby Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends jumper cables, telescopes, microscopes, cake pans, and nature/citizen-science kits (pollinator observing, stream mapping, weather, water-quality testing).
entries.hours: not listed on LoT page (see site)

entries.name: Birmingham Public Library (Central) — Library of Things
entries.description: Youth-and-family-oriented collection at the Central branch: sports kits, board games, Tonieboxes, sensory/STEAM/literacy kits, and outreach backpacks. Birmingham residents only. Guide last updated June 2026.
entries.address: Central Library, 2100 Park Pl, Birmingham, AL 35203
entries.hours: not listed on LoT guide (see site)

entries.name: Magic City ToolBank
entries.type: other (nonprofit tool bank — lends to organizations, not individuals)
entries.description: ToolBank USA affiliate warehouse lending tools and event/project equipment (projectors, screens, cooking equipment, hand and power tools) to nonprofits and community groups for volunteer projects.
entries.verifiedNote: site live and 2026 filings posted, though homepage images loaded poorly

entries.name: Alabama STAR / APTAT (Alabama Dept. of Rehabilitation Services)
entries.description: State AT Act program offering short-term loans of wheelchairs, scooters, communication devices, magnifiers, and other adaptive tech, plus five equipment-reuse centers statewide; no age or disability-type restrictions on reuse programs.

entries.name: Anchorage Tool Library (Seed Lab, Anchorage Museum)
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things / tool library
entries.description: Alaska's flagship tool library, housed in the Anchorage Museum's Seed Lab creative-reuse space; garden and home-improvement tools with sliding-scale memberships ($50 "Fix-it" / $75 "DIY" / pay-what-you-can) and reservation-based checkout. Featured in the Anchorage Daily News in June 2026.
entries.address: Seed Lab, 111 W 6th Ave, Anchorage, AK 99501

entries.name: Tucson Toolbox
entries.type: standalone tool library (501(c)(3) nonprofit, also a Library of Things)
entries.description: Description: A free-membership community tool library lending home-improvement, construction, lawn & garden, automotive, arts & crafts, and culinary equipment, with online reservations. Distinctively located inside a shopping mall, in the CATALYST Creative Collective space at Tucson Mall.
entries.address: Inside Tucson Mall (4500 N. Oracle Rd.), 1st floor, CATALYST Creative Collective, Tucson, AZ

entries.name: Phoenix Community ToolBank
entries.type: other (nonprofit tool bank — lends only to organizations, not individuals)
entries.description: Description: Warehouse-scale tool lending for nonprofits, schools, neighborhood associations, faith groups, and government agencies; borrowers pay a handling fee of 5% of retail value per week. Runs programs like "Heat Re-Leaf" and partners with the City of Phoenix on community projects.

entries.name: City of Phoenix Tool Lending Program (Neighborhood Services)
entries.description: Description: Free loans of cleanup tools, equipment, paint, and paint sprayers for neighborhood cleanup and beautification events. Open to neighborhood groups and Block Watches registered with the city's Neighborhood LINK.
entries.address: not published (city program; equipment reserved through Neighborhood Services)

entries.name: Mesa Public Library "Stuffbrary"
entries.description: Description: One of the metro area's best-branded Libraries of Things: bike repair kits and pumps, binocular/astronomy kits, blood pressure cuffs, cake pans, croquet sets, hiking kits with regional guides, park sports equipment, kids' play kits, Museum To-Go boxes, plus a seed library. Available at all Mesa Public Library locations (some items branch-specific).
entries.address: Main Library: 64 E. 1st St., Mesa, AZ (items at all branches)
entries.hours: not published on program page (varies by branch)

entries.name: Scottsdale Public Library — "So Much More!" collection
entries.description: Description: Lends board games and video games (PS4/PS5, Xbox, Switch), Citizen Science Kits, seasonal loom-knitting "Knit It Kits," children's Launchpad tablets, Book Club to Go kits, and Act One Culture Passes; also hosts a seed library.
entries.address: not published on program page (four branches; P.O. Box 1000, Scottsdale, AZ 85252)

entries.name: Prescott Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Lends telescopes, ukuleles (donated by the Prescott Ukulele Guild), hiking and pickleball kits, color-blindness glasses, Chromebooks, Wi-Fi hotspots, and culture passes, plus a seed library. Requires a Yavapai Library Network card.
entries.address: 215 E. Goodwin St., Prescott, AZ 86303 (Ask a Librarian Desk, 2nd floor)

entries.name: Prescott Valley Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Lends GoPros, musical instruments, podcast kits, a sewing machine, portable turntables and Bluetooth speakers, themed backpacks, games, Chromebooks, and hotspots — all free for ages 18+. Also runs Citizen Science Kits, memory kits, and a seed library.
entries.address: 7401 E. Skoog Blvd., Prescott Valley, AZ 86314 (pickup at Information Desk)

entries.name: Flagstaff City-Coconino County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Circulates a Library of Things (14-day loans) alongside board games, seeds, and culture passes; newly debuted Dementia Memory Kits in April 2026. Serves Flagstaff and Coconino County from downtown and east-side branches.
entries.address: 300 W. Aspen Ave., Flagstaff, AZ 86001 (Downtown Library)
entries.hours: not published on the pages checked (see site)

entries.name: AzTAP Assistive Technology Lending Program
entries.type: other (state assistive-technology loan library, run by Northern Arizona University)
entries.description: Description: Lends assistive technology devices to Arizonans with disabilities, families, and service providers for two-week home/school/work trials, with free shipping both ways; also administers a separate four-week loan library for Arizona schools. Site shows April 2026 news and events.
entries.address: 300 W. Clarendon Ave., Suite 475, Phoenix, AZ 85013

entries.name: Central Arkansas Library System — Library of Things & CALS Tool Library
entries.type: public-library LoT collection (with a dedicated tool library)
entries.description: CALS runs a full tool library out of its Dee Brown branch (automotive, woodworking, yard, masonry, electrical — up to 5 tools / 3 power tools per checkout, adults 18+), plus a system-wide Library of Things with telescopes, 100 mobile hotspots, birdwatching kits, and a seed library.
entries.address: Dee Brown Library, 6325 Baseline Rd, Little Rock, AR 72209 (tool library); other items system-wide
entries.hours: Tool checkouts by appointment during branch hours
entries.verifiedNote: page updated Apr 2026

entries.name: Saline County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: A broad LoT spanning tools & equipment, kitchen gear, outdoor/recreation items, hotspots, video games, hobby and health kits, plus a seed library and an in-house makerspace; patrons can suggest new items.
entries.address: Bob Herzfeld Memorial Library, 1800 Smithers Dr, Benton, AR 72015 (also Mabel Boswell Memorial Library, 201 Pricket Rd, Bryant)

entries.name: Bentonville Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends telescopes, GoPros, projectors, metal detectors, microscopes, laptops/iPads, and dozens of age-grouped discovery kits (robotics, coding, knitting, calligraphy) — even American Girl dolls; running since 2020.
entries.address: 405 S Main St, Bentonville, AR 72712 (smaller collection at 1101 SW Citizens Cir)

entries.name: Malvern–Hot Spring County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: A small-town LoT with kitchen equipment, bakeware, electronics, tools, toys, arts-and-crafts gear, and outdoor games; 1-week checkout with one renewal.

entries.name: Fayetteville Public Library — Center for Innovation
entries.type: other (public-library makerspace with reservable tools/equipment; tablets and Wi-Fi hotspots lent through outreach services)
entries.description: Not a checkout-and-take-home tool library, but FPL's Center for Innovation gives free reserved access to fabrication tools, tech, and studios, and the library lends tablets and portable Wi-Fi through its Lending Hands program. Included as the region's standout "beyond books" library service.
entries.hours: Library hours; tool/space use by reservation (programming on summer break through July 2026, reservations still open)

entries.name: Berkeley Public Library — Tool Lending Library
entries.description: One of the oldest tool libraries in the US (opened 1979); thousands of home-repair and garden tools lent free with a Berkeley library card from a dedicated building at the South Branch.

entries.name: Oakland Public Library — Tool Lending Library
entries.description: Free tool lending (thousands of tools) plus patron-driven workshops; serves Oakland, Emeryville, and Piedmont residents.

entries.name: Richmond Tool Library
entries.description: Free tool lending for Richmond residents run by the city's Recreation Department, MyTurn-powered; celebrating 10 years of service in July 2026.
entries.address: Richmond Recreation Complex, 3230 Macdonald Ave, Richmond, CA 94804

entries.name: Sonoma County Tool Library
entries.description: Free access to thousands of tools (yard, automotive, bicycle, woodworking, demolition) from inside the coLAB building; volunteer-staffed with reservation-based borrowing.

entries.name: Los Angeles Tool Library
entries.description: 100% volunteer-run tool library in the basement of Immanuel Presbyterian Church: power/hand/garden and specialty tools, 11-day loans, sliding-scale membership ($10–20/mo), plus a bike stand and workshops. 3300 Wilshire Blvd, Los Angeles, CA 90010 (parking at 675 S Berendo St)

entries.name: Sacramento Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of the earliest and most-cited public-library LoTs: GoPros, yard and home-care tools, musical instruments, and photography equipment reservable by card.
entries.address: Multiple branches (reserve via catalog); admin 828 I St, Sacramento, CA 95814

entries.name: Palo Alto City Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Beyond-books lending including bocce ball kits, book-club kits, and other gear across city branches; LoT roundup refreshed March 2026. 270 Forest Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301 (Downtown + branches)

entries.name: Contra Costa County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Distinctive lineup: C-Pen reading pens, home energy-efficiency kits, East Bay Regional Parks passes, early-literacy "Stories to Go" kits, and ukulele kits. 26 branches county-wide

entries.name: San Mateo County Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: Curated "joy and discovery" collection of interactive items free to borrow across county branches, complementing their makerspaces. Branches county-wide

entries.name: Santa Barbara Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Community passes, home energy kits, sewing and cooking equipment, educational toys, and try-before-you-buy items like Cricut machines.
entries.address: 40 E Anapamu St, Santa Barbara, CA 93101

entries.name: Santa Cruz Public Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: Bilingual (English/Spanish) LoT with telescopes (StarSense Dobsonians), Fire tablets, wifi hotspots and more; 3-week checkouts, plus a seed library in Felton. Branches county-wide

entries.name: LA County Toy Loan Program
entries.description: The country's oldest toy library — running since 1935 — letting kids borrow toys like library books at 60+ Toy Loan Centers; serves 30,000+ children a year, co-sponsored by LA County DPSS. Centers county-wide

entries.name: National City Public Library — "U-Tool-Ize" tool lending
entries.description: New (2025) hardware-lending program — car jacks, pole saws, bike repair kits, sewing machines — billed as the first of its kind in San Diego County (KPBS, Oct 2025).
entries.address: 1401 National City Blvd, National City, CA 91950

entries.name: Denver Tool Library
entries.description: Description: Member-based lending library with 5,000+ hand and power tools for home repair, lawn & garden, woodworking, metalworking, and arts, plus DIY classes and a community workshop. Annual membership is $150 with financial assistance available.

entries.name: Eco Tool Library at Eco-Thrift
entries.type: standalone tool library (community-run, housed at a thrift store)
entries.description: Description: Volunteer/community-run tool library lending tools, books, and kitchen gadgets on a low-cost membership (~$9/month), aimed at cutting overconsumption and improving equitable tool access. Runs on myTurn.
entries.address: 314 N Howes St, Fort Collins, CO 80521

entries.name: Lake County Public Library Tool Library & Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Free tool lending room (table saws, air compressors, drills, weed whackers, leaf blowers, heaters) for Lake County residents after a 15-minute orientation, created to tackle deferred home maintenance; also runs a separate Library of Things. Founded 2021 with CHFA grant, Bighorn Ace Hardware, and Cloud City Conservation Center support.

entries.name: Get Outdoors Leadville! Community Gear Library
entries.type: other (free outdoor gear library, Lake County Parks/Rec program)
entries.description: Description: Free outdoor gear lending for the Leadville community, part of a county program known for lowering barriers to outdoor recreation for local kids and families.
entries.address: CMC Timberline Campus, 901 US-24, Leadville, CO 80461

entries.name: Mountain Recreation Outdoor Gear Library
entries.description: Description: $10/month membership gear library lending paddleboards, PFDs, camping gear, snowshoes, avalanche safety gear, climbing/bike helmets, and fly-fishing equipment; up to 12 items per week.
entries.address: Edwards Field House, Edwards, CO (street address not published on program page)
entries.hours: sign-ups/pickups Tue 4–6pm; returns during facility hours

entries.name: Roaring Fork Gear Library
entries.description: Description: Free outdoor gear lending — paddleboards, kayaks, fishing gear, and bikes in summer; ski, snowboard, cross-country gear, snowshoes, and sleds in winter; camping gear year-round. Also gives away donated clothing.

entries.name: Denver Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Lends Chromebooks, hotspots, GoPros, projectors, home energy-monitoring tools (draft/power meters, air-quality monitors), bike repair kits, and Colorado State Parks passes across the 26-branch system.
entries.address: system-wide; Central Library, 10 W 14th Ave Pkwy, Denver, CO 80204 (some items Central-only)

entries.name: Arapahoe Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Large LoT spanning tools, sports and outdoor gear, digital media equipment, robotics/coding kits, assistive devices, board games, and video games; 3-week checkouts.
entries.address: multiple branches (district office: 12855 E Adam Aircraft Cir, Englewood, CO 80112)

entries.name: Longmont Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Lends tech (projectors, GoPros, karaoke machines), lawn games, sewing machines, telescopes, drill kits, car diagnostic scanners, and moving kits; 7-day, no-renewal checkouts.
entries.hours: return to Adult Reference Desk during open hours

entries.name: Lafayette Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Lends birding kits (Lafayette is a certified Bird City), home energy test tools, WiFi hotspots, memory kits for caregivers, book-group kits, and museum and state park passes.

entries.name: Summit County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Mountain-resort LoT launched May 2023 with ukuleles, pickleball sets, topo maps and hiking poles, SAD lamps, and Colorado State Parks and Quandary Peak parking passes; different items at each of the three branches.
entries.address: Main Library in Frisco plus branches in Breckenridge and Silverthorne

entries.name: Poudre Libraries — Gadgets & Things
entries.description: Description: "Why buy when you can borrow" collection: state parks passes and discover packs, light therapy lamps, blood pressure monitors, kitchen tools, ukuleles, karaoke machines, hotspots, Chromebooks, craft kits, and retro tech like Victrolas and videotape converters.
entries.address: multiple branches in Fort Collins area

entries.name: Pikes Peak Library District — Things to Borrow
entries.description: Description: Beyond-books lending includes free WiFi hotspots, checkout-able DSLR cameras, GoPros, tripods and light kits from its studios, video games, board games, Culture Pass museum admissions, and Colorado State Parks pass backpacks.
entries.address: system-wide across 15+ branches (flagship: Library 21c, 1175 Chapel Hills Dr, Colorado Springs)

entries.name: MakeHaven Tool Lending Library
entries.type: other (tool lending library inside a nonprofit makerspace)
entries.description: An extensive member-accessible tool library — miter saws, generators, compressors, concrete mixer, MIG welder, sewing machine — with free 7-day loans and a live availability directory. Lending is a benefit of MakeHaven membership.

entries.name: New Haven Free Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Citywide "beyond books" collection picked up at the Ives Squared Switchboard desk in the main library, spanning everyday essentials to specialized tools; borrowers sign a simple agreement (current version dated 2025).
entries.address: Ives Main Library, 133 Elm Street, New Haven, CT 06510

entries.name: Hartford Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Eight categories — arts & crafts, board games, cooking & baking (air fryer), health & wellness, home improvement, music, outdoors & garden, and tech — free with a card, one item per household, no late fees.

entries.name: The Westport Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Over 100 curated items from board games and musical instruments to a metal detector, displayed in a dedicated corridor on the main level. Guide last updated March 2026.

entries.name: Fairfield Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Newer, actively growing collection (page updated May 2026): lawn games, board games and puzzles at both locations, metal detectors and stud finders, with sewing machines and bike pumps announced as coming soon.

entries.name: Darien Library — Equipment & Gadgets
entries.description: Tech-leaning lending collection with an indoor air-quality monitor, Actron auto diagnostic scanner, Launchpads, and themed backpack and TEA (Technology, Engineering, Art) kits, each with posted loan periods and catalog checkout links.

entries.name: Simsbury Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Three-week loans on board games, puzzles, yard games, tools, and entertainment gear, plus a distinctive "Age Well" collection of games and activities designed for older adults with cognitive decline and their families.

entries.name: Public Library of New London — Library of Things & Toy Library
entries.type: public-library LoT collection (plus a children's toy library)
entries.description: LoT covers tech (hotspots, mini projector, podcast kit), cooking (air fryer), tools (laser level, Kill-A-Watt meters, tire inflator), and recreation (bocce, cornhole, fishing rods) on 7-day loans; the library also runs a separate Toy Library for kids.

entries.name: Ledyard Public Library — Toy Library
entries.description: A circulating toy collection at both the Bill Library and Gales Ferry branches, built with Traveling Toys, Inc. and funded by the Community Foundation of Eastern Connecticut, aimed at early-childhood play and easing costs for families.
entries.address: 718 Colonel Ledyard Hwy, Ledyard, CT 06339 (Bill Library); 18 Hurlbutt Rd, Gales Ferry, CT 06335

entries.name: Delaware Libraries — Library of Things (statewide)
entries.description: A single statewide catalog of gadgets, tools, games, and equipment borrowable with any Delaware library card: sewing machines, telescopes, garden and Dremel tools, musical instruments, blood pressure cuffs, hotspots, Chromebooks, STEAM kits, and induction cooktops. Items are checked out and returned at the same branch; Sussex County's library department promotes the same collection downstate.

entries.name: Dover Public Library
entries.description: Beyond the statewide catalog, Dover runs its own Seed Library (free locally-adapted seeds), a small Library of Things (e.g., American Girl Doll backpacks with one-week checkouts), and Little Free Libraries in seven city parks.

entries.name: Wilmington Public Library
entries.description: The state's biggest city library lends Chromebooks and mobile WiFi hotspots and offers free Longwood Gardens passes at the circulation desk, alongside access to the statewide Library of Things catalog.
entries.address: 10 E 10th St, Wilmington, DE 19801 (also North Branch, 3400 N Market St)

entries.name: DC Tool Library
entries.city: Washington, DC (16th Street Heights/Petworth area, Ward 4)
entries.description: Volunteer-run 501(c)(3), free for DC residents, lending woodworking, construction, and garden tools from a shed at Twin Oaks Community Garden (a second Ward 7 site at Lederer Gardens is indefinitely closed); active blog posts through late 2025.
entries.address: Twin Oaks Community Garden, 1380 Taylor St NW, Washington, DC 20011

entries.name: DC Public Library — Tool Library at the Labs (MLK Library)
entries.description: DCPL's tool-lending collection (launched Dec 2023) at the Labs in the MLK Memorial Library — hammers, wrenches, screwdrivers and more, reservable online — which has also absorbed the Event Supplies Library (projector/movie-night and block-party kits) for 7-day loans.
entries.address: Martin Luther King Jr. Memorial Library, 901 G St NW, Washington, DC 20001

entries.name: DPR Garden Tool Share Program
entries.type: other (city-run garden tool lending, with nonprofit partner Green Neighbors DC)
entries.description: DC Parks & Recreation lends garden and construction tools — rototillers, broadforks, power tools, even a cider press — to residents for up to a week during the growing season (March–November); operates in coordination with dctoollibrary.org.

entries.name: Orange County Library System — Library of Things
entries.description: Large, well-organized LoT: citizen-science kits (measuring light pollution, observing pollinators, ZomBee hunting), fiber arts/knitting/crochet kits, blood pressure toolkits, and tech items, free with an OCLS card.

entries.name: Dorothy Lumley Melrose Center (OCLS)
entries.description: 26,000 sq ft center at the Orlando Public Library with bookable audio/video/photo studios, makerspace, sound and podcast booths, VR/simulation lab, and equipment access — a standout "access over ownership" model.

entries.name: LeRoy Collins Leon County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Reservable non-traditional items picked up at the Main Library; 2-week loans, borrowers 18+, up to two concurrent reservations. Site shows July 2026 activity.

entries.name: Clearwater Public Library "Thingamabrary"
entries.description: Memorably named collection of tools, tech kits, games, and Wi-Fi hotspots; open to Clearwater cardholders and Pinellas Public Library Cooperative members, 7-day loans (14 for hotspots).

entries.name: Brevard County Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends ukulele kits for adults and kids (billed as Florida's first Library of Things ukulele loan program), American Heart Association blood pressure monitor kits, and other non-traditional items across branches.

entries.name: Manatee County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Binoculars, Wi-Fi hotspots, children's developmental toys, literacy kits, and museum/attraction passes; 3-week loans reservable through the catalog, returnable at any Manatee branch.

entries.name: Pasco County Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: Toys — including a notable adaptive-toy collection for kids with disabilities — plus electronics, hotspots, and seeds; 21-day renewable loans from branch service desks.

entries.name: SCF Libraries (State College of Florida) — Library of Things
entries.description: College library lending gadgets, A/V production gear, crafting supplies, fitness equipment, games, models, and tools; guide freshly updated June 2026.

entries.name: Mandel Public Library of West Palm Beach — Library of Things
entries.description: The city library's Library of Things page appears in current search listings under Browse & Borrow, but the site's firewall blocked every fetch attempt this session.

entries.name: Atlanta Community ToolBank
entries.description: Lends huge inventories of tools and equipment to nonprofits, schools, and community groups for volunteer projects (3% of retail value per week handling fee); part of the national ToolBank USA network. Not open to individual borrowers.

entries.name: Savannah Tool Library at Film Biz Recycling
entries.description: Bills itself as Savannah's first tool library: power drills, saws, wrenches, garden tools, and sewing machines, much of it donated by film/TV productions. Memberships start at $5/month with 7-day loans.

entries.name: Lekotek of Georgia
entries.city: Tucker (metro Atlanta, plus Alpharetta, Cobb, Duluth, Gainesville, Southside satellite sites)
entries.description: Toy lending library for children with disabilities: families attend facilitated play sessions and leave with a bag of adapted toys to borrow for the month; also runs computer clubs and teen programs.

entries.name: Sequoyah Regional Library System — Library of Things
entries.description: One of Georgia's fuller public-library LoT collections: sewing machine, tool set, stud finder, radon test kits, Wi-Fi hotspots, Chromebooks, disc golf sets, robots/STEM kits, ukuleles, and board games.
entries.address: 116 Brown Industrial Pkwy, Canton, GA 30114 (system HQ)

entries.name: Chattahoochee Valley Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: Browsable "Things" catalog across seven branches: GoPro video kits, blood pressure kits, musical instruments, STEAM kits, assistive tech (large-print keyboards), gardening items, and museum passes.

entries.name: Georgia PINES / Georgia Public Library Service — statewide Library of Things
entries.description: A free PINES card works at ~300 Georgia public libraries, many of which lend LoT items (ukuleles, cake pans, sewing machines, kits); GPLS actively promotes the program with posts as recent as 2026.

entries.name: HNL Tool Library (Re-use Hawai'i)
entries.description: Honolulu's tool library, operated by deconstruction/reuse nonprofit Re-use Hawai'i from its Salt Lake warehouse (newly reopened location in 2026); garden, home-improvement and DIY tools with monthly workshops. Memberships $20/mo, $50/3mo, $150/yr.
entries.address: 4561 Salt Lake Blvd, Suite 102, Honolulu, HI 96818

entries.name: North Kohala Tool Library
entries.description: Volunteer-run rural tool library in the old Sunshine Hardware building lending construction, crafting, cleaning, and catering "tools" mostly free; recently expanded membership to Waikoloa, Waimea, Honoka'a, and Kawaihae, with new hours effective June 15, 2026.

entries.name: Creative Confluence Tool Library
entries.description: Description: Membership-based community tool library run by Salmon Valley Stewardship, lending hand and power tools plus oddities like a poultry-processing trailer. The only classic standalone tool library operating in Idaho (it's the state's sole entry on the Tool Library Alliance map).

entries.name: Ada Community Library — Gear Collection
entries.description: Description: Library-of-Things-style "Gear Collection" spanning Learn at Home, Outdoor, Play, STEAM, Tech and Wellness categories, checked out with a library card across the district's Victory, Lake Hazel, Star and Hidden Springs branches.
entries.address: 10664 W Victory Rd, Boise, ID 83709 (Victory Branch; three other branches)

entries.name: Meridian Library District — Kits
entries.description: Description: Large borrowable "Kits" collection including DSLR cameras, projectors, GoPro accessories, portable power stations, video-to-digital converters and Kill A Watt energy meters; the district also runs unBound, a dedicated technology branch.
entries.address: 1268 W Orchard Park Dr, Meridian, ID 83646 (Orchard Park; also Pinnacle and unBound at 722 E 2nd St)

entries.name: Community Library Network — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Seven-branch North Idaho district with a formal Library of Things (borrower agreement required, items checked out at circulation desks) plus a big "Full STEAM Ahead" kit collection and classroom STEAM kits for educators. Agreement form updated May 2026.
entries.address: branches in Athol, Harrison, Hayden, Pinehurst, Post Falls, Rathdrum, Spirit Lake

entries.name: Coeur d'Alene Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: City library with a named Library of Things "bookshelf" alongside Chromebook/hotspot lending and a seed library.
entries.address: 702 E Front Ave, Coeur d'Alene, ID 83814

entries.name: Latah County Library District — The Curiosity Collection
entries.description: Description: LoT-style "Curiosity Collection" of maker kits, tech and devices, puzzles/games, Itty Bitty Brain Boxes and Voyager (outdoor exploration) kits; also runs the Moscow Seed Library and a recurring Repair Café.
entries.address: 110 S Jefferson St, Moscow, ID 83843 (plus six rural branches)

entries.name: Boise WaterShed — Lending Library
entries.description: Description: Boise's water-education center lends free kits and models — aquifer models, Enviroscape watershed models, home/school water-audit kits and water-quality testing kits — to educators and community members for two-week periods.
entries.hours: kits reserved via online form; facility hours not published on lending page

entries.name: Idaho Assistive Technology Project (IATP) — Device Lending Libraries
entries.type: other (assistive-technology lending library, University of Idaho program)
entries.description: Description: Statewide program letting Idahoans test and borrow assistive-technology devices for free through lending libraries at resource centers across the state.
entries.address: not published (resource centers across Idaho; based at University of Idaho, Moscow)

entries.name: The Chicago Tool Library
entries.city: Chicago (East Garfield Park; founded in Bridgeport)
entries.description: Thousands of tools for DIY, home repair, gardening, camping, cooking, and crafts — drills to sewing machines to ice cream makers — on a pay-what-you-can membership. Active with 2026 board, new Sunday hours, and ongoing repair fairs/workshops.
entries.address: 4015 W. Carroll Ave, Suite 101, Chicago, IL 60624

entries.name: Tool Library of Lake County
entries.description: 3,000+ tools (carpentry, sanders, drills, lawn mowers) on MyTurn; $50/year membership, free repair clinics, new-member orientation.

entries.name: West Bloomington Revitalization Project Tool Library
entries.description: Hand and power tools, yard equipment, automotive, kitchen, and woodworking tools; donation-based basic membership (suggested $35) or $50 premiere with workshops; up to 7 tools out at a time.

entries.name: Carbondale Tool Library
entries.description: Free community tool lending run out of the Rainbow Cafe; browsable MyTurn inventory, borrowing now by appointment/reservation with a volunteer.
entries.address: 118 N. Illinois Ave (inside Rainbow Cafe), Carbondale, IL 62901
entries.verifiedNote: site live, though low-activity

entries.name: Chicago Public Library — Library of Things (Uptown Branch)
entries.description: CPL's dedicated LoT at the Uptown branch: carpet cleaner, pasta maker, sous vide, popcorn and bubble machines, telescope, cornhole; 1-week loans, in-person checkout only, collection still growing.

entries.name: Skokie Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Longstanding, wide-ranging collection: adaptive tools/toys, photo-video-audio equipment, musical instruments, STEM kits, home-maintenance tools, and kids' kits.

entries.name: Oak Park Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: DIY (drills, laser level, sewing/embroidery machines), entertainment (karaoke, telescope), outdoor (tiller, bocce, utility wagon), and tech (Adobe-loaded laptops, GoPros, hotspots); 3-week loans, 18+, Main Library lobby desk only.

entries.name: Arlington Heights Memorial Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Growing collection from board games and hotspots to cameras, sewing machines, and game controllers; AHML cardholders only, with patron suggestions welcomed.
entries.address: 500 N. Dunton Ave, Arlington Heights, IL 60004

entries.name: Indian Prairie Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Home-repair, crafting, and tech items across eight categories (incl. media converters); 1-week loans, 2 items per cardholder, $1/day late fee.

entries.name: West End Tool Library
entries.description: Garage-based neighborhood tool share run by Evanston's West End Area Block Club (the Evanston Rebuilding Warehouse itself no longer advertises a tool library — it's a reclaimed-building-materials warehouse).
entries.verifiedNote: (site live but last update 2019)

entries.name: Indianapolis Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Pilot program (Sept 15, 2025–Sept 14, 2026) lending arts & crafts gear, assistive devices, garden and household tools, musical instruments, and outdoor rec items (sewing machines, fishing poles, pickleball sets). Offered at six branches (College Avenue, Decatur, East 38th St, Fort Ben, Southport, West Perry), expanding system-wide through 2026; 21-day checkout, 2 items.

entries.name: South Bend Tool Library
entries.description: Nonprofit tool library (program of ContainArt Community Corp., 501(c)(3)) lending construction, home-repair, woodworking, metalworking, and gardening tools to South Bend residents 18+; pay-what-you-want annual membership, 7-day loans.
entries.verifiedNote: site copyright 2022–2026

entries.name: Monroe County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends ukulele kits, baking kits, RPG kits, video-production kits, iPads, hotspots, energy monitors, toys, and a seed library; pickup at Downtown Library, Ellettsville Branch, or Bookmobile.
entries.address: 303 E. Kirkwood Ave, Bloomington, IN 47408 (Downtown Library)

entries.name: Evansville Vanderburgh Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Big "borrow instead of buy" collection across 15+ categories — notably kayaks and paddleboards (with safety waiver), plus DIY tools, garden tools, instruments, baking gear, STEAM kits, and cultural passes; general waiver required.
entries.address: multiple branches (Central: 200 SE Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd)

entries.name: Hussey-Mayfield Memorial Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends STEM/robotics kits, maker kits, sensory bags (weighted blankets, noise-cancelling headphones), cornhole sets, baking pans, hotspots, laptops, museum passes, and a ~100-variety seed library.

entries.name: Johnson County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Broad collection: STEAM and memory kits, board games, instruments, lawn games, sewing machines, metal detectors, blood-pressure monitors, light-therapy lamps, Enchroma color-blindness glasses; mostly 3-week checkouts.

entries.name: Indiana University Library of Things
entries.description: IU's guide to gear borrowable across campus centers: bikes, kitchen equipment, AV gear, sports/outdoor equipment, games, and repair/garden tools; primarily for IU students/affiliates.

entries.name: Indy Tool Lending Library (iTooLL)
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things / tool co-op
entries.description: Co-op lending audio, video, creative, and event-production equipment at ~$1/tool/week, hosted by Indy Art | Media Co-op and the Kheprw Institute.
entries.verifiedNote: (site live but newest posted update is 2016; current operations unclear)

entries.name: Allen County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: ACPL maintains a Library of Things page and is cited by the Indiana State Library as running a LoT program, but page contents couldn't be confirmed via fetch.
entries.address: 900 Library Plaza, Fort Wayne, IN 46802 (Main Library)
entries.verifiedNote: (page exists but details not retrievable)

entries.name: Salvage Barn Tool Library (Friends of Historic Preservation)
entries.description: Free-to-borrow hand and power tools for home maintenance, DIY, and restoration projects, run alongside the architectural-salvage Salvage Barn; 2-week loans for members 18+.
entries.address: 2401 Scott Blvd SE, Iowa City, IA

entries.name: Matthew 25 Tool Library
entries.description: No membership fee; small hand tools borrow free and big items (drywall jacks, tile saws, ladders) go out for a low daily fee, with inventory on myTurn and safety training on request.
entries.address: 201 Third Ave SW, Cedar Rapids, IA (alley entrance on 2nd St SW)

entries.name: Greater Des Moines Habitat for Humanity Tool Lending Library
entries.description: 400+ tools loaned 3 days at a time to owner-occupant homeowners; $195/yr membership with a $25 income-qualified rate, based at the East Euclid ReStore.
entries.address: 2200 E Euclid Ave, Des Moines, IA 50317

entries.name: Public Space One — Art Equipment Lending Library
entries.description: Community art center lending art tools and equipment free to PS1 members for one-week loans; a distinctive arts twist on the tool-library model.
entries.address: 538 S Gilbert St, Iowa City, IA 52245

entries.name: Des Moines Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: 50+ items including home-improvement tools, party supplies, board games, and memory-preservation gear (e.g., digitizing equipment); 3-week loans shipped from Central to any branch. Central Library: 1000 Grand Ave, Des Moines

entries.name: Cedar Falls Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Musical instruments, GoPros, hotspots, Chromebooks, outdoor games, gardening tools, STEM kits, and a notable historical doll kit collection; 2-week renewable loans.

entries.name: Scott County Library System — "Beyond the Book"
entries.description: Broad non-book collection across all branches: telescopes, sewing machines, hotspots, Rokus, ukuleles, light-therapy lamps, outdoor games, and many themed kits; mostly 3-week fine-free loans. Branches: Eldridge, Blue Grass, Buffalo, Durant, Princeton, Walcott + bookmobile

entries.name: Iowa City Public Library — "More to Borrow"
entries.description: ICPL's non-book lending program (its "More to Borrow" page is live in current search listings), but the site blocked automated fetches so contents couldn't be confirmed directly.
entries.address: 123 S Linn St, Iowa City, IA
entries.verifiedNote: (site blocked fetch)

entries.name: University of Iowa Lichtenberger Engineering Library — Tool Library
entries.description: Lends hand tools, measuring tools, 3D scanners, cameras, programming/circuit kits, and VR/AR gear from the Engineering Library service desk, primarily for the campus community.
entries.address: Lichtenberger Engineering Library, University of Iowa, Iowa City

entries.name: Wichita Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of the region's broadest LoTs: anatomical models, binoculars and bird-watching kits, blood-pressure monitor kits, Chromebooks, museum/attraction Experience Passes, Finch robots, memory kits (dementia support), microscopes, radon detectors, telescopes, and Tonieboxes; 2 items at a time.
entries.address: Advanced Learning Library, 711 W 2nd St N, Wichita, KS 67203

entries.name: Topeka & Shawnee County Public Library
entries.description: Added telescopes in summer 2026 (partnership with the Astronomical League of Kansas) on top of an established nonbook lineup: arts & craft kits, bird-watching kits, cake pans, conversation kits, health bags, oral history kits and Book Group in a Bag.

entries.name: Lawrence Public Library
entries.description: Lends an Audio-Visual Tool collection from its SOUND+VISION Studio — VHS digitization kits, VCR-to-USB adapters, slide/film scanners, audio recording gear — plus a Seed Library.

entries.name: Louisville Tool Library
entries.description: Volunteer-run 501(c)(3) tool lending library modeled on a book library; lends hundreds of power tools, hand tools, and household equipment on a 7-day cycle, with sliding-scale annual membership. Catalog runs on myTurn; profiled by Louisville Public Media as recently as May 2026.

entries.name: The Lexington Tool Library (LexTools)
entries.description: Community-owned, pay-what-you-can library lending power tools, hand tools, kitchen equipment, camping gear, and lawn/garden tools out of the ArtFarm site; currently fundraising for a "mobile LexTools" unit.

entries.name: Empower Tools
entries.description: Free home-improvement tool library run by Kenton County Public Library with the Center for Great Neighborhoods, housed in the Hellmann Creative Center; free 7-day loans (up to 3 tools) with a KCPL card.
entries.address: 321 W. Martin Luther King Jr. Blvd, Covington, KY 41011

entries.name: Kenton County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Separate from Empower Tools: 14-day loans of telescopes, metal detectors, ladders, wheelbarrows, hand trucks, moving kits, yard games, and an oral history recording kit across KCPL branches.

entries.name: Louisville Free Public Library
entries.description: Standout non-book lending at scale: telescopes and binoculars checkable at every LFPL location, plus sensory kits, book-discussion kits, and reservable makerspace equipment (3D printers, laser cutters, sewing/embroidery machines, recording gear).
entries.address: Main Library, 301 York St, Louisville, KY 40203 (system-wide)

entries.name: Jessamine County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of Kentucky's earlier and better-developed public LoTs: musical instruments, sewing machines, Chromebooks, Wi-Fi hotspots, telescope, metal detector, birdwatching kits, a VASCO language translator, plus games, puzzles, and a microscope; most items lend for 3 weeks.

entries.name: Bullitt County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: 100+ items at all five BCPL locations: camping gear, metal detector, binoculars, fitness and gardening equipment, kitchen gadgets (fondue pot, induction cooktop), karaoke machine, projector, and lawn games; takes public suggestions for new items.
entries.address: Central Library, 740 Conestoga Pkwy, Shepherdsville, KY 40165 (plus 4 branches)

entries.name: McCracken County Public Library — "Things to Borrow"
entries.description: Western Kentucky's standout: fishing rod-and-reel combos (via KY Dept. of Fish & Wildlife), disc golf and pickleball kits, blood pressure monitor kits (via the local health department), mobile hotspots, bike locks, and a seed library.

entries.name: Toys with a Purpose
entries.description: Free lending library of adapted toys and books for children of all abilities, affiliated with the University of Kentucky; loans run up to 3 months and items ship anywhere in Kentucky at no cost.

entries.name: KDLA Kit Lending Library (Kentucky Dept. for Libraries & Archives)
entries.description: myTurn-powered statewide collection of book-discussion kits and programming kits (books, electronics, games, toys) available to any Kentucky resident, reserved through local public library staff; 9-week loans shipped to your library.

entries.name: Lafayette Tool Library
entries.description: Free tool borrowing for any Lafayette resident — power and hand tools for home, yard, and auto maintenance, stocked largely through community donations.
entries.verifiedNote: site live; footer dated 2023, recent local news coverage

entries.name: Broadmoor Tool Lending Library (Broadmoor Improvement Association)
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things / community tool library
entries.description: Free neighborhood tool lending run by the Broadmoor Improvement Association on the MyTurn platform; membership limited to residents of the 70125 ZIP, 2 rentals per checkout, 1-week loans.
entries.address: Broadmoor Arts & Wellness Center, 3900 General Taylor St, New Orleans, LA 70125
entries.hours: Pickup by reservation, weekdays and Saturdays
entries.verifiedNote: live MyTurn inventory

entries.name: New Orleans Public Library — Tool Lending (CleanUp NOLA)
entries.description: Cleanup-focused tool lending at the East New Orleans Regional branch — shovels, rakes, wheelbarrows, and gloves for neighborhood and catch-basin cleanups; 2 items per patron, 7-day loans.
entries.address: East New Orleans Regional Library, 5641 Read Blvd, New Orleans, LA 70127
entries.verifiedNote: page updated Apr 2026

entries.name: East Baton Rouge Parish Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of the state's biggest LoTs: Arduino kits, board games, telescopes, musical instruments, art prints & statues, blood pressure kits, digital microscopes, Wi-Fi hotspots ("Wi-Fi 2 Geaux"), state park and museum passes, plus seed libraries.
entries.address: Main Library at Goodwood, 7711 Goodwood Blvd, Baton Rouge, LA 70806 (items across branches)

entries.name: Livingston Parish Library — Library of Things (LOTs)
entries.description: Lends learning kits, hobby kits, board games, iPads/Launchpads, celebration kits, and cultural passes across its branches.

entries.name: Edith Garland Dupré Library — Library of Things (UL Lafayette)
entries.description: University library collection lending games, kitchen items, school supplies, tech, engineering/architecture gear, and even stuffed animals to UL Lafayette students.
entries.address: 400 E St. Mary Blvd, Lafayette, LA 70503

entries.name: Maine Tool Library
entries.description: Nonprofit membership tool library circulating gardening, landscaping, home-improvement, and kitchen tools via an online MyTurn catalog; volunteer-run with quarterly volunteer trainings.

entries.name: Maine GearShare
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things (outdoor gear library)
entries.description: Lends and rents outdoor gear (sleeping bags, packs, trekking poles, rain gear, skis, skates, winter clothing) to individuals, schools, and nonprofits, plus repair services; also convenes the statewide Maine Gear Library Network of 40+ member organizations.

entries.name: Katahdin Gear Library (Outdoor Sport Institute)
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things (outdoor gear library)
entries.description: Free borrowing of mountain and fat-tire bikes, canoes, kayaks, paddleboards, cross-country skis, snowshoes, and backpacks; membership is free with a library card from one of five Millinocket-area libraries.
entries.hours: by appointment (seasonal; summer hours announced on social media)

entries.name: Kindling Collective
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things (outdoor gear library)
entries.description: Queer-centered gear library and education center at the Equality Community Center in Portland's Arts District; sliding-scale seasonal memberships (free for BIPOC members) cover camping, hiking, backpacking, winter, and canoe-tripping gear for up to two weeks per loan.

entries.name: Skowhegan Outdoors Basecamp Gear Library (Main Street Skowhegan)
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things (outdoor gear library)
entries.description: 200+ item community gear library (XC skis, snowshoes, bikes, canoes, SUPs, camping gear, winter clothing) free for Somerset County residents, with paid memberships for other Mainers and visitors; also runs free outdoor programming.

entries.name: Teens to Trails
entries.type: other (gear lending to school outdoor clubs)
entries.description: Statewide nonprofit that loans and gives donated camping and camp-kitchen gear to Maine school outdoor clubs (not general-public lending); runs gear drives and passes clothing and boots directly to students.

entries.name: Curtis Memorial Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of Maine's largest public-library Things collections (roughly 1,500 gadgets, tools, kitchen and outdoor items per 2025 news coverage), plus Chromebook/hotspot kits and Maine GearShare passes.

entries.name: Portland Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Nontraditional lending collection browsable in the library's catalog alongside park and museum passes; part of an active five-branch city system.
entries.hours: Mon 10am–5pm; Tue–Wed 10am–6pm (see site for full schedule)

entries.name: Lithgow Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Friends-funded collection including snowshoes (adult and youth), a telescope, GoPro, projectors, power stations, sewing machine, mobile hotspots, coding kits, and VHS digitizing gear.

entries.name: Freeport Community Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends craft tools, air-quality and electricity monitors, telescope, binoculars, Nintendo Switch, digital cameras, hotspots, board games, and American Girl dolls; page updated June 2026.

entries.name: Berwick Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Unusually adventurous small-town collection: inflatable kayaks, 6-person tents, snowshoes, sleds, power tools, sewing machine, Cricut, nature-explorer backpacks, plus loaner guitars and ukuleles via Continuum Arts Collective.
entries.address: 103 Old Pine Hill Rd., Berwick, ME 03901

entries.name: Jackson Memorial Library — Tool Lending Library
entries.description: Midcoast village library lending kitchen gear (blenders, specialty pans, butane torch), hand tools, and power tools (drills, impact drivers); up to 10 tools per checkout with a free library card.

entries.name: Station North Tool Library
entries.description: Baltimore's flagship nonprofit tool library, lending 3,000+ tools to members and running roughly 30 classes (woodworking, home repair, crafts) plus a public woodworking open shop. One of the best-known tool libraries on the East Coast.

entries.name: Baltimore Community ToolBank
entries.type: other (nonprofit tool bank serving organizations, not individuals)
entries.description: A ToolBank USA affiliate lending 10,000+ tools and pieces of equipment to 750+ member nonprofits, schools, faith communities, and neighborhood groups for service projects; a typical tool order costs about $45.

entries.name: Rockville Tool Library
entries.description: Membership-based tool library on MyTurn for home repair, hobby, and garden tools, with an onsite makerspace; open to Montgomery County residents 18+ ($60/yr standard, sliding scale to $12; $15/mo option).
entries.hours: Pickup Tue & Thu 6–8pm, Sat 12–4, Sun 2–4; onsite use Sat 12–4

entries.name: Enoch Pratt Free Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Citywide LoT with pickup at any Pratt branch: Chromebooks and hotspots, a Children's Library of Things (ages 0–8), STEAM to Go kits, and a Teen Library of Things with coding robots, sewing machines, digital microscopes, and gaming devices.

entries.name: Baltimore County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of Maryland's broadest collections, 20+ categories: 70+ board games, camera kits, engravers, karaoke kits, induction cooktops, adventure backpacks, memory kits — plus quirky branch specialties like fishing poles at North Point and musical instruments at Catonsville.

entries.name: Anne Arundel County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends C-Pen ReaderPens (assistive text-to-speech scanning pens), ukulele starter kits, and Memory Lab equipment for digitizing home movies, records, photos, negatives, and slides.

entries.name: Howard County Library System — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends framed artwork, a DIY collection (repair, sewing, gardening, cooking tools), sewing machines, ukuleles, STEM kits, memory care kits, video games, and educational toys, plus a seed library; available at Central, East Columbia, Elkridge, Glenwood, Miller, and Savage branches.

entries.name: Rockville Public Library (Rockville Memorial Library) — Library of Things
entries.description: City library collection of about 19 items — bocce and giant dice sets, ukulele, tool kit, laser measure, crochet/knitting kits, a typewriter, and a video converter; one-week checkouts at the circulation desk (page updated Jan 2026).

entries.name: Montgomery County Public Libraries — Library of Things / musical instrument lending
entries.description: MCPL launched its Library of Things in 2018 with a musical instrument lending collection based at the Twinbrook branch (202 Meadow Hall Dr, Rockville); the county's 2018 announcement was fetched successfully, but the program's current page could not be reached this session to confirm 2026 status.

entries.name: Somerville Tool Library
entries.description: Volunteer-run lending library of carpentry, electrical, and gardening tools housed at the Parts and Crafts makerspace; $50/year membership lets you borrow up to 7 tools a week, no Somerville residency required.

entries.name: Cape Cod Toy Library
entries.description: Lends from a collection of 2,000+ non-electronic toys, games, and puzzles for kids up to age 8, plus a play space; membership from $30/month with financial assistance available.

entries.name: Boston Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: A rotating, system-wide collection of non-traditional items (14-day loans) picked up at participating branches with a physical BPL/MBLN card; the library maintains a public wishlist for new "Things."
entries.address: 700 Boylston St, Boston, MA 02116 (Central Library; items at participating branches)

entries.name: Somerville Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Categories include circulating tech (Chromebooks, hotspots, Nintendo Switch), kitchen equipment, lawn games, instruments, home-monitoring meters, health aids, birding kits, and ELL learning kits; 2-week loans for ages 13+.

entries.name: Robbins Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of the Minuteman network's best-known LoTs: kitchen gadgets, tech, games, crafts, outdoor equipment, musical instruments, event supplies, and home-improvement tools on 2-week loans.

entries.name: Newton Free Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Large browsable online catalog of tools, sewing machines, STEAM to-go kits, outdoor gear, and technology, mostly on renewable 3-week loans — pitched as "try before you buy."

entries.name: Public Library of Brookline — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends Chromebooks, digital projectors, graphics tablets, iPads with assistive-technology apps, games, kitchen items, and maker tools across its branches, mostly 2-week renewable loans.

entries.name: Wellesley Free Library — Library of Things
entries.description: An ever-expanding collection of non-traditional items — bed prism glasses, digital microscopes, a toolkit and more — plus a separate children's Library of Things.

entries.name: Berkshire Athenaeum — Library of Things
entries.description: The strongest Library of Things in Berkshire County (no standalone "Berkshire Tool Library" could be found): ukuleles and other everyday and not-so-everyday items lent from the reference desk and children's library, funded by the Friends of the Berkshire Athenaeum.

entries.name: Ann Arbor District Library — "Unusual Stuff to Borrow" / Tools collection
entries.description: The best-known "things" collection in the state: telescopes, synthesizers, art prints, science kits, yard games, and a "Book a Tool" reservation system for events — all free with an AADL card, picked up Downtown.
entries.address: 343 S. Fifth Ave., Ann Arbor, MI 48104 (Downtown pickup)

entries.name: E. Warren Tool Library
entries.type: standalone tool library (nonprofit, run by Motor City Grounds Crew)
entries.description: Free tool checkout for MorningSide-area residents plus mobile tool libraries, safety training, and open shop hours geared to block clubs and neighborhood cleanups; org memberships $150–350. Heavy positive local coverage in May 2026 (WDIV tool drives).

entries.name: Carhartt Workshop — Tool Bench
entries.description: Free tool lending for Detroit and surrounding communities from Carhartt's Midtown workshop, with online reservations, a 20-minute new-user orientation, and "Sharpen Your Skills" workshops.

entries.name: Detroit ToolBank
entries.type: other (nonprofit tool bank — lends to organizations)
entries.description: Opened April 2026 with backing from Ford Philanthropy and Carhartt; lends construction, landscaping, cleanup, and event equipment to nonprofits, schools, faith groups, and neighborhood organizations.

entries.name: Ypsilanti District Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Well-organized LoT spanning tools, tech, kitchen, crafts (button makers, Cricut), medical devices, music, and outdoor games; higher-risk "Tier 2" items need a one-time waiver, and patrons can suggest new Things.
entries.address: 5577 Whittaker Rd., Ypsilanti, MI 48197 (Whittaker Road branch)

entries.name: Traverse Area District Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Gadgets, tools, and games including a full musical-instrument lineup (guitars, synths, even a theremin), telescopes, metal detectors, birding backpacks, sewing machines, and home-energy meters.

entries.name: Kent District Library — "KDL Beyond Books" collection
entries.city: Grand Rapids area (18 branches, HQ Comstock Park)
entries.description: Large "Beyond Books" collection of high-cost items — stargazing binocular kits, trekking poles, film-to-digital converters, metal detectors, tire inflators, mahjong sets — transferable between branches. (Grand Rapids Public Library itself has no branded LoT; KDL is the area standout.)
entries.address: items float among KDL's branches; 814 West River Center Dr. NE, Comstock Park, MI 49321 (service center)

entries.name: Capital Area District Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: LoT plus a "LOT Jr." for kids (Launchpads, American Girl dolls), board games, Raising Reader backpacks, and a "ThingsMap" showing which branch holds what; adults sign a borrowing agreement.
entries.address: 401 S. Capitol Ave., Lansing, MI 48933 (Downtown; Things across 13 branches)

entries.name: Royal Oak Public Library — LoT (Library of Things)
entries.description: Separate adult and youth collections: laser levels, tire-change kits, radon detectors, sewing/knitting machines, theremini and other instruments, projectors, karaoke machines, pickleball sets; 3-week loans with two renewals.
entries.address: 222 E. Eleven Mile Rd., Royal Oak, MI 48067

entries.name: Kalamazoo Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: "Library of Things" is a top-level catalog category on KPL's site, and local press (NowKalamazoo, July 2025) covers the beyond-books lending; an April 2026 Wayback snapshot shows the collection live.

entries.name: Minnesota Tool Library

entries.name: Twin Cities Library of Things
entries.description: Volunteer-run LoT founded by two local moms; ~750 items (kitchen appliances, tools, camping gear, party supplies, instruments, baby gear) with no-contact self-service pickup via myTurn.

entries.name: Minnesota Toy Library
entries.description: Members check out up to 5 toys for 2–4 weeks from three church-hosted branches; aimed at reducing waste and toy clutter while supporting child development. Locations: 8 W 60th St, Minneapolis 55419; 2510 Cleveland St NE, Minneapolis 55418; 1505 Burns Ave, St. Paul 55106

entries.name: One Roof Community Housing Tool Lending Library
entries.description: Free tool lending open to all local residents for home improvement and repair, up to one week per loan; reserve online, pick up with ID and borrower agreement.

entries.name: Duluth Community Garden Program Tool Library
entries.description: Members borrow gardening tools plus food-processing gear — canners, dehydrators, apple peelers/corers, juicers, sausage makers — and the program gives away hundreds of donated canning jars each summer.

entries.name: Ramsey County Library — Library of Things

entries.description: Longstanding "things" lender (framed art since the 1970s); current collection spans crafts, cooking, games, outdoor gear, and tools with 3-week, renewable checkouts.

entries.name: Stillwater Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends telescopes, birding kits, nature backpacks, VR headsets, laptop/hotspot kits, and memory-care kits; mostly 3-week loans picked up in person.

entries.name: Scott County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: County system LoT confirmed via its live listing in search results, but the county site rate-limited all fetch attempts so current details couldn't be confirmed.

entries.name: Hancock County Library System — Library of Things

entries.name: Warren County-Vicksburg Public Library — Library of Things

entries.name: Southern Miss Outdoor Adventures Gear Rental
entries.description: Low-cost rental of camping and backpacking gear — tents, packs, sleeping bags/pads, stoves, cook sets (e.g., 2-person tent $7/day) — through USM Campus Recreation; community eligibility not stated.

entries.verifiedNote: page live, last updated 2025

entries.name: Cultivate KC — Urban Agriculture Tool Library
entries.description: Lends garden and small-farm tools to urban farmers, community gardeners, and residents via a Lend Engine online catalog; relaunched and appointment-based.
entries.address: 4049 Pennsylvania Ave, Ste 206, Kansas City, MO

entries.name: River City Outdoors — Gear Lending Library
entries.description: Free outdoor-gear lending (1,000+ pieces — camping, paddling, etc.) for St. Louis City/County residents and community organizations; short online training, then reserve on myTurn.

entries.name: Shaw Tool Library
entries.description: Free donation-based tool-sharing cooperative running since 2020; no membership — you email to coordinate a loan from the pegboard collection of hand and power tools.
