entries.name: Laverne Schell Tool Library (Community Partnership of the Ozarks)
entries.description: Long-running program (often cited as a statewide model) lending home-repair and gardening tools — up to 5 tools per week — plus quarterly Fix-It Fairs; $30/yr membership.
entries.address: O'Reilly Center for Hope, 1518 E Dale St, Springfield, MO

entries.name: St. Louis County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Free checkout of musical instruments, outdoor recreation gear, accessibility tools (low-vision/dyslexia), health-monitoring devices, projectors, puzzles/games, and STEAM kits. HQ: 1640 S Lindbergh Blvd, St. Louis, MO (branch availability varies)

entries.name: University City Public Library — Take-Home Technology
entries.description: Lends 70+ Chromebooks, 50+ hotspots, Sony camcorders, and Orion telescopes with an adult card; 1–3 week loans.

entries.name: Springfield-Greene County Library District — hotspot & telescope lending
entries.description: District-wide Wi-Fi hotspot lending (3-week loans, deliverable to any branch) plus checkout telescopes at four branches added in 2025.

entries.name: Cherokee Street Tool Library
entries.description: Neighborhood tool library on Cherokee Street with a myTurn catalog (tools plus oddities like event games) that still shows hours and inventory — but its main website domain is dead and Yelp marked it closed in April 2026, so current status is doubtful.
entries.verifiedNote: (conflicting signals; possible closure)

entries.name: MUD Project Tool Library (Missoula Urban Demonstration Project)
entries.description: Description: Montana's flagship tool library, lending 3,500+ tools for home, garden, yard, and automotive projects; most tools are free with a MUD membership, with small fees on high-maintenance equipment. Online reservations run on myTurn (mud.myturn.com).

entries.name: Missoula Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Deep "things" collection: telescopes, induction cooktops, automotive diagnostic scanners, kill-a-watt meters, kids' life jackets, birding backpacks, an extensive cake-pan collection, board games, plus a seed library and culture passes to local museums.

entries.name: Lewis & Clark Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: 14-day loans of telescopes, birdwatching kits (funded by Last Chance Audubon), blood-pressure kits done in partnership with the state health department, C-Pen reader pens, Bluetooth audio gear, and themed kits, returnable at any branch.
entries.address: 120 S. Last Chance Gulch, Helena, MT 59601

entries.name: MonTECH (University of Montana Rural Institute)
entries.description: Description: Lends assistive technology and adaptive recreation equipment free to any Montanan for 30/90/180 days (up to 12 items), with free shipping statewide; browsable online inventory with self-serve checkout requests.
entries.address: University of Montana campus, Missoula; MSU-Billings College of Education, Billings
entries.hours: Missoula Tue–Fri 8:30–4 (Mon by appt); Billings by appointment

entries.name: Free Cycles Missoula
entries.type: other (nonprofit community bike shop / bike lending)
entries.description: Description: Long-running community bicycle shop where anyone can use stands, tools, and donated parts to build or fix a bike for free; a bike-world cousin of a tool library and a Missoula institution since 1996.

entries.name: Slow Spokes Gear Library
entries.description: Description: All-volunteer group lending complete bikepacking kits (bags, camping gear, etc.) free of charge to trans, non-binary, gender-expansive, and women riders in the Missoula area; profiled by Bikepacking Roots in Feb 2026.

entries.name: MCAT (Missoula Community Access Television) Equipment Lending
entries.description: Description: Free checkout of cameras, tripods, lighting, and microphones to community producers who hold a Missoula Public Library card and complete a short training; borrowers contribute content to MCAT's public-access channels.
entries.address: 455 E. Main St., Missoula (based inside Missoula Public Library)
entries.hours: trainings Sat 10am and Mon 6:45pm; lending by arrangement

entries.name: Deer Camp (Hellgate Hunters & Anglers)
entries.description: Description: Women-founded gear library lending donated hunting equipment (packs, optics, clothing) to new and underrepresented hunters, run through the local Hellgate Hunters & Anglers chapter; drew statewide press when it launched.
entries.verifiedNote: (page still exists but is script-rendered and its current lending status couldn't be confirmed as of July 2026)

entries.name: Omaha Public Library
entries.description: System-wide (12 locations) special collections: the Discovery Pass program (free reservable passes to Omaha museums and attractions, plus pop-up event passes), a Toys & Kits collection, Book Club Bags, and the Common Soil Seed Library. Multiple locations; hours vary by branch

entries.name: Gretna Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: A dedicated "nontraditional catalog" in a fast-growing Omaha suburb: ukuleles, cake pans, a telescope, board games and more, plus attraction Activity Passes; LoT items have their own Biblionix catalog view and must be returned in person.
entries.address: Main Library, 736 South St, Gretna, NE 68028

entries.name: Boulder City Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: The most complete Library of Things in southern Nevada: garden tools (with waiver), car OBD code scanners, hiking GPS units, hiking/creature-discovery backpacks, STEAM and storytime kits, cake pans, board games, hotspots, and binge boxes. Partly funded by IMLS.

entries.name: Carson City Library
entries.description: Description: Lends NASA Explorer backpacks, VR headset kits, STEM kits, audio and camera equipment, preloaded Kindles, Chromebooks and MacBooks, and Book Club in a Bag sets (some items require a "Premier Card"). No overdue fines.
entries.address: 900 N. Roop St., Carson City, NV 89701

entries.name: Las Vegas–Clark County Library District — Museum & Park Passes
entries.description: Description: Cardholders check out free admission passes: DISCOVERY Children's Museum family passes, Springs Preserve family passes, Horses4Heroes ranch visits, and a Nevada State Parks Pass covering day-use entry to all 27 state parks. Available at the information desk of every branch.

entries.name: Henderson Libraries (Henderson District Public Libraries)
entries.description: Description: Beyond books, lends Wi-Fi hotspots, Chromebooks, jigsaw puzzles, framed art prints, Book Clubs in a Bag, and the Nevada State Park Pass across its branches.
entries.address: not published on collection pages (multiple branches)

entries.name: City of Las Vegas Tool Lending Library
entries.description: Description: A city-run trailer stocked with leaf blowers, power washer, hedge trimmers, rakes, shovels, wheelbarrow, mechanic tool set, and safety gear, delivered by Neighborhood Services to community cleanup sites. Borrowers must be 18+, show ID, and belong to a registered neighborhood association, HOA, or Adopt-A-Spot group within city limits.
entries.address: not published (mobile trailer; city contact at 495 S. Main St., Las Vegas, NV 89101)

entries.name: Washoe County Library System — Park Passes & Museum Passes
entries.description: Description: Each branch holds two checkable Nevada State Park Passes (free day-use entry to all 27 state parks, vehicle up to 8 people), plus Discover & Go museum passes; also offers 3D printing. Checkout is in person only.
entries.address: branches system-wide; Downtown Reno branch: 301 S. Center St., Reno, NV

entries.name: The Gear Library (Bethlehem Trails Association / Bike the North Country)
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things (outdoor gear library)
entries.description: Free self-serve outdoor gear borrowing from unstaffed sheds at three White Mountains locations (Bethlehem Elementary School, the Littleton rail trail, the Franconia Inn): bikes, helmets, and hiking equipment reserved online with emailed access codes.

entries.name: Seacoast Stewardship Tool Library (Nature Groupie / UNH Cooperative Extension)
entries.description: Free MyTurn-powered lending of trail-work and conservation tools (shovels, pruning saws, pry bars, gloves) to conservation organizations, towns, and volunteer groups across the Seacoast; run with the Southeast Land Trust of NH.

entries.name: Concord Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends tools and gadgets (thermal camera, Kill A Watt meters, laser level), media-digitizing equipment (film/slide scanners, VHS and vinyl converters), recreation gear (telescope, metal detector, karaoke machine, pickleball set), and instruments (guitar, ukulele).

entries.name: Nashua Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: All-ages Things collection (tools to Blu-ray players) with a distinctive peg-board browsing system in three spots around the library; 21-day loans, curbside pickup available, plus a seed library and puzzle exchange.

entries.name: Dover Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Deep collection spanning 30+ board games, craft equipment (looms, screen printing, sewing machine), outdoor games, tools, health devices (blood-pressure monitor, ECG, pulse oximeter), and a separate children's Things collection with robots and a Yoto player.

entries.name: New Hampshire State Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Statewide backbone collection — Nature Explorer packs, OSMO learning games, accessible youth materials, STEM kits, traveling kitchen — that circulates to any NH public library, which then lends to local patrons and schools.

entries.name: Gorham Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Small North Country collection with early-learning kits, borrowable toys, puzzles, and an NH Astronomical Society loaner telescope, alongside curated Mt. Washington hiking resources.

entries.name: New Hampshire Toy Library Network
entries.description: Nonprofit (founded 2019) building community toy-lending in the Durham/Seacoast area via pop-ups and partnerships; active Instagram/Facebook presence but no functioning standalone website could be fetched, so current lending status could not be confirmed.
entries.verifiedNote: UNVERIFIED

entries.name: Sustainable Camden County Tool Library
entries.description: County-run tool lending library serving Camden County residents (Cherry Hill area) with an online myTurn reservation catalog of home-improvement and yard tools.

entries.name: Sustainable Princeton Lending Library
entries.description: Free two-week loans of electric landscape equipment, induction cooktops, and repair tools to Princeton residents and nonprofits — a "try before you electrify" sustainability project sponsored by NRG Energy.

entries.name: Princeton Public Library Library of Things
entries.description: One of NJ's best-known LoTs: laptops, hotspots, binoculars, portable power stations, emergency weather radios, nature-explorer backpacks, and Little Thinker kids' kits; the page also cross-promotes local partner lending programs.

entries.name: Hoboken Public Library Library of Things
entries.description: Lends home improvement tools, musical gear, and video game consoles for home checkout, alongside the library's separate technology-lending and MakerSpace programs.

entries.name: Montclair Public Library Library of Things
entries.description: Telescope, microscope, ukulele starter kit, video-to-digital converter, projector/mic, plus new hands-on science kits (biodiversity, water-quality monitoring); holds placed by phone or email, pickup at Main or Bellevue Avenue branch.

entries.name: Plainsboro Public Library — Plainsboro Library of Things (PLoT)
entries.description: Newer LoT funded by an NJ State Library "Level Up Your Library" grant, lending equipment and tools alongside the library's seed library and makerspace.

entries.name: Hunterdon County Library Library of Things
entries.description: County-system LoT lending crafting tools, electronics, home tools, and recreation items free to resident cardholders (up to a set number of Things for 14 days), sponsored by the Friends of the Hunterdon County Library; pickup at Headquarters.

entries.name: Cherry Hill Public Library Library of Things
entries.description: Large LoT spanning iPads, hotspots, a portable record player, musical instruments, memory kits, and hobby/craft/board-game collections, each with its own loan rules; complements the town's county tool library.

entries.name: Santa Fe Habitat for Humanity ReStore Tool Lending Library
entries.type: nonprofit tool library (housed at the Habitat ReStore)
entries.description: Description: Borrow up to 2 power tools and 3 hand tools at a time for 7 days on a $38/year membership open to Santa Fe County residents; proceeds support Habitat's affordable-housing mission.
entries.address: Santa Fe Habitat ReStore, 2520 Camino Entrada, Ste. A, Santa Fe, NM 87507

entries.name: UNM ECSC Toy Lending & Resource Libraries
entries.city: statewide (Albuquerque, Española, Gallup, Las Cruces, Lovington, Roswell)
entries.description: Description: Free toy, educational-material, book, and video lending for parents and caregivers at six sites across New Mexico, run by UNM's Early Childhood Services Center — all you need is an ID. Also sets up "Paloma's Play Corners" in family-service agencies.
entries.address: Albuquerque flagship: 5816 Isleta Blvd SW, Albuquerque, NM 87105 (other addresses listed on site)

entries.name: Las Cruces Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: LoT at the Thomas Branigan Memorial Library lending "from baking supplies to traditional fiber art tools and everything in between," launched with a state Creative Industries grant; paired with the library's makerspace.
entries.address: Thomas Branigan Memorial Library, 200 E Picacho Ave, Las Cruces, NM 88001
entries.hours: not published on program page (library info: 575-528-4005)

entries.name: Vista Grande Public Library
entries.description: Description: Small community library that lends telescopes, microscopes, bird-watching kits, baking pans, iPods, WiFi hotspots, puzzles, and museum passes alongside books.

entries.name: Santa Fe Farmers' Market Institute Tool Library
entries.description: Description: Tool lending program for Santa Fe Farmers' Market vendor-farmers, still listed among the Institute's farmer programs alongside microloans, but its dedicated program page now returns a 404, so current status is unclear.
entries.address: 1607 Paseo de Peralta, Suite A, Santa Fe, NM 87501 (Institute office)
entries.verifiedNote: UNVERIFIED (program page removed; only a passing mention on the live site)

entries.name: The Tool Library
entries.description: Volunteer-powered nonprofit lending 5,000+ tools — power tools, lawn equipment, automotive and tech gear — for a modest annual membership; celebrated its 15th anniversary in 2026 and runs lead-safe renovation trainings with Erie County.

entries.name: Brooklyn Public Library Tool Library
entries.description: Free tool lending at BPL's Greenpoint Library and Environmental Education Center; borrow up to three tools (one power tool) for a week — cordless saws, jigsaws, heat guns, hand tools — born out of BPL's Repair Hub project.
entries.address: 107 Norman Ave at Leonard St, Brooklyn, NY 11222

entries.name: Brooklyn Public Library Library of Things
entries.description: System-wide "more than books" lending: telescopes, board games, musical instruments (the first instrument lending library in NYC, at Central Library), museum passes, and tools.

entries.name: SEAC Tool Shed
entries.description: Tool lending library run by the South East Area Coalition, open to residents of a nine-county Greater Rochester region for a small annual fee; a mobile lending unit rotating through city R-Centers is rolling out.
entries.address: 1255 University Ave, C010 (lower level), Rochester, NY 14607

entries.name: Historic Albany Foundation Tool Library
entries.type: standalone tool library (program of a preservation nonprofit)
entries.description: 500+ items — pressure washers, shop vacs, power and garden tools — geared toward old-house repair; $20/year membership ($15 for HAF or Albany Public Library members), borrow up to 4 items for two weeks.

entries.name: Flatbush Tool Library (Flatbush Mixtape)
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things / standalone tool library
entries.description: Free membership and free borrowing of hammers, wrenches, and other hand and household tools for any Brooklyn resident 18+; operates out of a storage unit, access by appointment.

entries.name: Green Guerillas Tool Library
entries.description: Lends power tools, string trimmers, hedge trimmers, and hand tools free to members of NYC community gardens (not home gardeners), with twice-monthly delivery to gardens; operates seasonally mid-March through October.
entries.hours: Office pickup Mon–Fri 10am–4pm (scheduled 3+ days ahead), mid-Mar–Oct

entries.name: Smithtown Library Library of Things
entries.description: Long Island standout lending guitars, coding robots (Bee-Bot, Botley), air quality monitors, photo-to-USB scanners, bocce and badminton sets, and retro game consoles; two items at a time for a week.

entries.name: Levittown Public Library Library of Things
entries.description: Broad LoT collection (Bluetooth speakers, bocce sets, tech and recreation gear) circulating 14 days, tied to the library's Innovation Station makerspace.

entries.name: Asheville Tool Library
entries.description: Volunteer-powered, membership-based nonprofit lending over 3,700 tools — one of the largest tool libraries in the Southeast; members browse and reserve via a MyTurn inventory.

entries.name: Charlotte ToolBank (Charlotte Community ToolBank)
entries.type: other (ToolBank USA affiliate — nonprofit-to-nonprofit tool lending)
entries.description: Lends tools and equipment in bulk to nonprofits, schools, and charitable groups for volunteer projects (not individual borrowers), part of the national ToolBank USA network.

entries.name: Raleigh Tool Share (City of Raleigh)
entries.description: Free lending of battery-electric lawn equipment (EGO mowers, string trimmers, blowers) to Raleigh residents for non-commercial use; reserve online, pick up at the Raleigh Pathways Center.

entries.name: Neighborhood Toolbox Program (City of Greensboro)
entries.description: Free tool library for Greensboro residents and community groups, lending everything from rakes and shovels to lawn mowers and plumbing snakes for up to five days; run by the city with Lowe's as a partner.
entries.address: Greensboro Sportsplex parking lot, 2400 16th St., Greensboro, NC

entries.name: Keep Durham Beautiful Tool Lending Library
entries.description: Lends garden and cleanup tools (shovels, pruners, wheelbarrows, electric mower, litter grabbers, event recycling bins) free to Durham-based community groups and organizations for service projects.

entries.name: Tool Lending Program, N.C. Cooperative Extension — Forsyth County
entries.description: Free two-week loans of garden tools to community members (refundable deposit required), run by NC State Extension's Forsyth County office; program page updated Dec 2025.

entries.name: New Hanover County Public Library "Library of Things"
entries.description: Take-home kits across arts & crafts, education, games, health & wellness, outdoors, and emergency preparedness; cardholders borrow one kit at a time for 3 weeks. Site shows current July 2026 notices.
entries.address: Main Library, 230 Grace Street, Suite A, Wilmington, NC 28401

entries.name: Durham County Library — Maker + STEAM kits and tool kits
entries.description: The Main Library's Innovation Lab checks out STEAM kits (Ozobots, Sphero, electronics repair) plus practical tool kits (stud finder, laser level, OBD code reader, air quality monitor); covered by WRAL as Durham's "Library of Things."
entries.address: Main Library, 300 N. Roxboro St., Durham, NC

entries.name: Hocutt-Ellington Memorial Library "Library of Things"
entries.description: Growing non-traditional collection: electronics and technology, board games, outdoor activity gear, early-literacy backpacks, Launchpad tablets, plus a seed library and puzzle exchange; adult cardholders can borrow 2 kits + 1 tech item.

entries.name: Gaston County Public Library "Library of Things"
entries.description: Launched Sept 2024; lends specialty items like cake pans and games for one-time projects and "try before you buy" — free with an adult library card.

entries.name: NC State University Libraries Technology Lending
entries.type: other (academic library equipment lending; campus community only)
entries.description: One of the country's largest academic device-lending programs — laptops, cameras, audio gear, AR/VR headsets, and gaming equipment across five campus libraries; limited to the NC State community, but a notable regional model.

entries.name: Fargo Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Kits, equipment and "experiences" alongside books: adventure backpacks, board games, curiosity kits, disc golf kits, Launchpads, mobile hotspots, ND State Park passes, radon detector kits, draft/power check meters and Sunspotter telescopes. Main Library, 101 4th St N, Fargo, ND 58102 (also at Dr. James Carlson and Northport branches)

entries.name: Minot Public Library — Tool Library + Makerspace Checkout
entries.type: public-library LoT collection (includes the state's first tool library)
entries.description: MPL runs what it calls North Dakota's first Tool Library (yard, plumbing and construction tools; 3 at a time, 18+, waiver required) plus checkout-to-home makerspace gear (GoPro, Spheros, LEGO Mindstorms, projectors), 150+ board games, Roku sticks/Tonies/Launchpads, and ND State Park passes.

entries.name: Bismarck Veterans Memorial Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: ND State Park and Gateway to Science passes, disc golf and pickleball sets, kids' life jackets, fishing poles (tackle included, yours to keep), board/video games, WhaZoodle speakers, Launchpads and radon detector kits.

entries.name: Grand Forks Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Tech-leaning collection: green screens, umbrella lighting, podcast microphones, 3D-printing pens, karaoke and bubble machines, outdoor movie screen, wireless printer, light-therapy lamps; one-week loans for adult cardholders.

entries.name: Modcon Living Tool Library
entries.description: The country's original tool library, founded 1976 (formerly the Rebuilding Together Central Ohio Tool Library — the old rtcentralohio.org site is stale; Modcon Living is the current name). Lends 1,225+ hand and power tools for home repair, DIY, and community gardens; annual membership $50 ($25 students/seniors/veterans), with income-based and nonprofit tiers. Runs on MyTurn.

entries.name: Home Repair Resource Center Tool Library
entries.type: standalone tool library (program of a home-repair nonprofit)
entries.description: Hundreds of hand and power tools, most free to borrow with a $25/yr membership (eligibility limited to its service area); online catalog with reservations. Site links a 2025 annual report and current policies.
entries.hours: Mon–Wed 12–6 (closed federal holidays and Thanksgiving–New Year's)

entries.name: Toledo Tool Library
entries.type: standalone tool library (program of Bluff Street Village at the Monroe Street Neighborhood Center)
entries.description: Lends home, garden, and yard tools from screwdrivers to wheelbarrows and electric drills (no gas-powered equipment); recently renovated with a $200K Lowe's grant. Active local news coverage May 2025 (WTVG).

entries.name: The Well CDC Community Tool Library
entries.description: 500+ tools — power, hand, plumbing, carpet, lawn & garden, electrical, roofing — open to Summit County and nearby residents; $50/yr "Akronite" membership with $20–40 discounted community tiers. Currently accepting new members (MyTurn signup).

entries.name: UpCycle Ohio Tool Library
entries.type: nonprofit tool library (part of Rural Action's UpCycle Ohio thrift store/makerspace, in partnership with Athens County Public Libraries)
entries.description: Borrow-don't-buy tool lending for weekend projects with an online inventory portal; attached to a community makerspace and mending service. Site copyright 2026.
entries.hours: not published for tool library (makerspace hours on site)

entries.name: Cincinnati ToolBank
entries.type: other (nonprofit tool bank — lends to organizations, not individuals)
entries.description: Year-round tool and equipment lending for nonprofits, schools, neighborhood associations, and faith/civic groups at nominal fees; site features a 2025 Impact Report. (Note: I could not find any live site or archive for a member-facing "Cincinnati Tool Library" in Northside — it appears defunct or never had a web presence; this is Cincinnati's active tool-lending org.)

entries.name: Akron-Summit County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Multiple LoT collections: circulating art, cake pans, memory kits, STEM/educational kits, kitchen tools, hotspots, and "Lucky Day" family passes to local attractions; clear loan-period grid on the page.
entries.address: 60 S. High St., Akron, OH 44326 (Main; some items at branches)

entries.name: Stark Library — Library of Things / "Unexpected Things"
entries.description: Dedicated Library of Things within a broader "Unexpected Things" lineup that includes Stark Parks Packs, DIY "Golden Gear" kits, and Adventure Passes.

entries.name: Westlake Porter Public Library — Non-Traditional Materials
entries.description: One of NE Ohio's deepest "things" collections: 8mm-film and cassette digitizers, Bee-Bot robots, LEGO sets, board/card games, and lots of equipment/technology, all holdable in the catalog.

entries.name: Dayton Metro Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Main site is live and the collection got a fresh Dayton Daily News photo feature on July 9, 2026, but the library keeps the collection in its catalog and I could not confirm a dedicated LoT webpage.
entries.verifiedNote: (site loads; LoT collection page could not be confirmed)

entries.name: Eastern Oklahoma Library System — Library of Things
entries.city: Muskogee (15 branches across eastern OK: Tahlequah, Grove, Eufaula, Sallisaw, etc.)
entries.description: The biggest true Library of Things in the state: sewing machines, looms, heat presses, guitars, ukuleles, karaoke machines, projectors, cameras, fishing poles, cornhole, dumbbells and more, checked out for 21 days from any of 15 branches (return to the same branch).
entries.address: system-wide; HQ in Muskogee, OK (per-branch pickup)
entries.verifiedNote: page live, last updated Sept 2025

entries.name: Metropolitan Library System — Metro Grows garden tool lending + Discovery Backpacks
entries.description: Launched spring 2024 with garden tools (shovels, spades, rakes, loppers, broad forks, hand-tool sets) checkable at branches across the OKC system, plus themed nature "Discovery Backpacks" for kids 4-12; the current page says each branch stocks the same tool set.
entries.address: system-wide, 19 branches (OKC, Edmond, Midwest City, Bethany, etc.)

entries.name: Lawton Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: A 40+ item collection for adult cardholders (2-week checkout): accordion, keyboard, electric drum set, GoPro, digital camera, projector, metal detector, disc golf/croquet/bocce sets, blood-pressure monitor, cold-therapy unit, color-blind glasses, bird-watching and gardening kits.

entries.name: Oklahoma ABLE Tech — Device Loan Program
entries.type: other (statewide assistive-technology lending library, run through Oklahoma State University)
entries.description: Lends from an inventory of ~5,000 assistive-tech devices (communication, hearing, vision, mobility, daily living, recreation) — up to 3 devices for 6 weeks, free, to Oklahomans with disabilities, families, and professionals.
entries.address: 1514 W Hall of Fame, Stillwater, OK 74078
entries.verifiedNote: page last modified Sept 2025

entries.name: Ability Connection Oklahoma
entries.type: other (adapted toy & equipment lending; listed as Oklahoma's only USATLA toy library)
entries.description: Long-running nonprofit (UCP affiliate) that gives or loans durable medical equipment (wheelchairs, walkers, strollers, bathroom equipment) at no cost and provides sensory/adapted toys for children with disabilities.
entries.address: 10320 S. Penn Ave, Ste 204, Oklahoma City, OK 73159
entries.verifiedNote: live site fetched; equipment-loan program listed on current What We Do page

entries.name: OKC Tool Library (Oklahoma City Tool Library Inc.)
entries.description: Volunteer nonprofit tool-lending effort with a live MyTurn checkout portal, but the inventory is closed to browsing, it has no standalone website, and no 990 filings or recent independent activity could be found — current operating status unclear.
entries.verifiedNote: (portal loads but shows no address/hours/inventory; social accounts only)

entries.name: ToolShare Community Tool Lending Library
entries.description: New Tulsa initiative giving residents "easy access to a wide range of tools and equipment, ready to borrow, within communities" (per its LinkedIn profile); its only web presence is a Facebook page, which could not be fetched to confirm 2026 activity.
entries.verifiedNote: (Facebook-only presence; LinkedIn page live in 2026 but no recent independent coverage)

entries.name: Northeast Portland Tool Library
entries.description: Long-running volunteer 501(c)(3) lending tools free to NE Portland residents of all income levels; hosted at the Leaven Community Center.

entries.name: North Portland Tool Library
entries.description: One of Portland's original free neighborhood tool libraries, operating out of the historic Kenton Firehouse; donation-based, Saturdays only. (Note: its old northportlandtoollibrary.org domain is now squatted — the MyTurn site is the live presence.)

entries.name: Southeast Portland Tool Library (SEPTL)
entries.description: Free hand- and power-tool lending for residents inside its SE Portland boundary; 7-day loans, all-volunteer.

entries.name: SW Portland Tool Library
entries.description: Newer all-volunteer library serving SW/Downtown/NW/South Portland from a green shipping container at Greater Portland Bible Church; household and garden tools.

entries.name: East Portland Tool Library
entries.description: 100% volunteer-operated free tool lending for east Portland residents; operates from the Cherry Park United Methodist Church parking lot.

entries.name: Green Lents Community Tool Library
entries.description: Free tool library for 11 SE/East Portland neighborhoods, run by sustainability nonprofit Green Lents alongside a seed library and bilingual (English/Spanish) programming.

entries.name: Rockwood Common Tool Library
entries.type: standalone tool library / other (tool truck + lending lockers)
entries.description: Free, first-come tool lending from a "Tool Truck" at the Rockwood Repair & Reuse Center, plus 24/7 lending lockers and mending circles; broad inventory from kitchen items to automotive tools. 18600 SE Stark St, Gresham/Rockwood, OR

entries.name: Hillsboro Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of the best-known public-library LoTs in the country: hundreds of items (kitchen gear, tools, tech, outdoor and hobby equipment) borrowable free with a library card; the city actively crowdsources new item ideas.
entries.address: Hillsboro Brookwood Library, 2850 NE Brookwood Pkwy, Hillsboro, OR 97124

entries.name: Eugene Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Cameras, sewing machines, robotics kits, musical instruments, games, and educational toys across Downtown, Bethel, and Sheldon locations; actively expanding into new categories. 100 W 10th Ave, Eugene, OR 97401 (Downtown + branches)

entries.name: ToolBox Project
entries.description: Eugene's longtime neighborhood tool lending library. Its website (toolboxproject.org) was unreachable with broken DNS as of July 2026, so current status is unclear — verify locally before listing.
entries.verifiedNote: (site could not be fetched, July 2026; possibly defunct)

entries.name: West Philly Tool Library
entries.description: Long-running nonprofit lending library with 4,000+ tools for home repair, gardening, and DIY, on sliding-scale memberships from $20/year with no per-tool fees. Relocated from West Philly to Grays Ferry in late 2025 ([coverage](https://www.westphillylocal.com/2025/11/17/we-tried-our-best-west-philly-tool-library-finds-new-home-in-grays-ferry/)).

entries.name: PHS McLean Urban Agriculture Tool Library
entries.type: nonprofit Library of Things (garden/ag tool library)
entries.description: Pennsylvania Horticultural Society program lending garden and grounds tools free to registered community gardens and growing sites; 7-day loans, seasonal March–November (2026 season opened Feb 28).
entries.hours: seasonal, reservation and walk-up windows; Sat by reservation only

entries.name: Tacony Tool Library
entries.description: Community tool library in Northeast Philly (launched 2020) lending hand and power tools through an online myTurn catalog.
entries.address: 5200 Unruh Ave, Section J, Philadelphia, PA 19135

entries.name: Rutabaga Toy Library
entries.description: Zero-waste toy lending library and play space for kids 0–8, with memberships, a scholarship fund, and toy donation program; in mid-2026 it is actively moving to a bigger space at 3301 Scotts Lane.
entries.address: 3426 Conrad St, C2, Philadelphia, PA 19129 (moving to 3301 Scotts Lane)

entries.name: Free Library of Philadelphia — Musical Instrument Collection
entries.description: The Free Library's Music Department lends guitars, ukuleles, keyboards, drum machines and other instruments free with a library card — one of the best-known instrument-lending collections in the US.

entries.name: Millvale Tool Library (Millvale Community Library)
entries.type: standalone tool library (housed at a community library)
entries.description: Lends 1,000+ hand and power tools — table saws, pressure washers, mowers, ladders — free with a Pittsburgh/Allegheny County library card (five tools at a time, one week); recently expanded with a RAD grant.

entries.name: Pittsburgh Toy Lending Library
entries.description: Volunteer-run cooperative since 1974 (Mister Rogers filmed there in 1986) with an indoor play space, public play sessions ($7/child drop-in), and a lending collection of 400+ toys for members.
entries.hours: public play sessions Mon, Tue, Thu, Sat (volunteer-dependent; see calendar)

entries.name: Library of Things — Carnegie Library of Pittsburgh / Allegheny County libraries
entries.description: Shared county catalog of borrowable "things": pickleball paddles, ukuleles, synthesizers, pottery wheels, sewing machines, pressure washers, tile saws, Cricuts, lawn games, and retro-to-current game consoles.

entries.name: Cooper-Siegel Community Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of the deepest LoT collections in the region: karaoke machines, turntables, projectors, yogurt makers, dozens of cake pans, hiking/birding kits, mahjong sets, power stations, and more, with new items added regularly.

entries.name: Harrisburg (HBG) Tool Library
entries.description: New 501(c)(3) (opened late 2025) where neighbors borrow power tools, garden gear, and household items on sliding-scale memberships ($0–$100/yr); volunteer-run with workspace and training.

entries.name: Central PA Tool Library & Makerspace
entries.description: Membership tool library serving the Lebanon County area, lending power tools, hand tools, and garden basics via a myTurn catalog, plus makerspace programming.

entries.name: Lancaster Workshop + Tool Library
entries.type: standalone tool library / other (workshop + tool library)
entries.description: Opened 2026: lends professional-grade tools and teaches trades classes (stained glass, plaster, carpentry) with an intergenerational mentorship bent; catalog runs on Lend Engine.

entries.name: Lancaster Public Library — Specialty Lending
entries.description: Distinctive lending collection including American Girl dolls, harps (Mountville branch), ukuleles, explorer bags, board/outdoor games, cake pans, hotspots, and museum passes.

entries.name: Altoona Area Public Library — Tool Lending
entries.description: Launched Sept 2024 with ~40 items — sewing machines, power washers, lawn mowers, hedge trimmers, socket sets — free to Blair County cardholders ([WPSU coverage](https://radio.wpsu.org/2024-09-19/altoona-area-public-library-launches-free-tool-lending-program-for-patrons)).

entries.name: PVD Things
entries.description: A 501(c)(3) cooperative lending library in Olneyville with 1,400+ items — power tools, electronics, camping and outdoor games — and around 425 members. One of the few true co-op libraries of things in New England.

entries.name: PVD Things Tool Library at Providence Public Library
entries.type: public-library LoT collection (run in partnership with PVD Things)
entries.description: A curated tool-and-things shelf inside the downtown library: cordless drills, sander, laser level, stud finder, multimeter, plus a projector, telescope, fishing rod, and tennis racket. Free with a library card, checked out at the Information Services desk. Page updated May 2026.

entries.name: Westerly Library & Wilcox Park — Library of Things
entries.description: A deep, well-documented collection: birdwatching kit (with Cornell birdsong guide), metal detector, radon detector, post hole digger, moving dollies, mini projector, and lawn games like Putterball. Each item has a detailed description and its own borrowing policy.

entries.name: Warwick Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Broad collection at the Central branch ranging from a Cricut heat press, sewing machine, and crochet/knitting kits to a Bounty Hunter metal detector and even a ghost-hunting kit (EMF meter, spirit box, EVP recorder). Each listing links to live availability in the catalog.

entries.name: Barrington Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: "Borrow the unexpected": Orion telescope kit, GoPro HERO9, Epson projector, Garrett metal detector, pickleball set and more, mostly on 7-day loans from the circulation desk.

entries.name: North Providence Union Free Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Smaller but active collection of Kindles, Launchpad electronic puzzle games, mobile hotspots, jigsaw puzzles, and a telescope.
entries.address: 1810 Mineral Spring Ave, North Providence, RI 02904

entries.name: Richland Library "Library of Things"
entries.description: The state's flagship public-library LoT: equipment and useful items for projects, home repair, and outings, available at 12 locations across the system (Main, Ballentine, Cooper, North Main, Blythewood, Sandhills, and more).

entries.name: Anderson County Library System — LEAP Station
entries.description: "Learn, Experience, Adventure, Play" stations lend pickleball and disc golf sets, fishing rods, metal detectors, birdwatching kits, microscopes, tablets, STEAM kits, and state park/museum passes; expanded in 2025 to Main, Pendleton, Iva, Powdersville, and Belton branches.

entries.name: Greenville County Library System — board games, seeds & passes
entries.description: No formally branded LoT, but lends tabletop board games (Hughes Main Library, 14-day loans), heirloom seeds (Berea branch and Bookmobile), and museum/state park passes with a library card.

entries.name: Charleston County Public Library — non-traditional lending
entries.description: Cardholders can borrow mobile Wi-Fi hotspots plus other non-traditional items such as telescopes and digital magnifiers; site is active, though the lending page describing these items dates from 2019, so confirm current availability.

entries.name: York County Library — non-book lending
entries.description: Modest "things" offering: reminiscence kits (for dementia caregivers), mobile hotspots, and state park passes; site is current with summer 2026 programming.
entries.address: 138 E Black St, Rock Hill, SC 29730

entries.name: Toy Lending Library of South Dakota
entries.description: Ten-year-old nonprofit lending free curated toy boxes for children 0–5 to promote learning through play — 1,800+ toy boxes, 44K+ checkouts, and 21 lending locations statewide including many Siouxland Libraries branches.
entries.address: 401 S Spring Ave, Sioux Falls, SD 57104

entries.name: Siouxland Libraries — Special Collections
entries.description: "More than books" lending across 13 city/county locations: board games, STEM Discovery Kits, Stories to Go storytime kits, Adventure and Reading Kits, Wonderbooks, Book Club to Go bags, and Toy Lending Library boxes (in partnership with the nonprofit above). Downtown Library, 200 N Dakota Ave, Sioux Falls, SD 57104; hours vary by branch

entries.name: Brookings Public Library
entries.description: Circulates board games, yard games, maker kits, Launchpads, Tonieboxes & Tonies, video games and SD State Park passes alongside books.

entries.name: Rapid City Public Library
entries.description: No branded Library of Things; circulates board games and offers a well-equipped in-library Makerspace (3D printers, vinyl cutter, etc.) plus in-house laptop checkout.

entries.name: Chattanooga Public Library — "Beyond Books" collections (Tool Library, Adventure Library, Toy Library, Noise Closet & more)
entries.type: public-library LoT collection (the strongest in the state)
entries.description: Umbrella of non-book collections: a Tool Library (hand/power/garden tools via the 4th Floor Makerspace, sponsored by Elder's Ace Hardware), an Adventure Library (tents, sleeping bags, trekking poles from Outdoor Chattanooga; pickleball/weight/yoga kits from the YMCA), a Toy Library (Tonies, Magna-Tiles, Montessori sets, microscopes), a Noise Closet (guitars, drum kits, keyboards, amps), telescopes (Eastgate branch), tabletop games, and memory-care kits. Tools: 3 at a time, 1 week, waiver required.
entries.address: Downtown Library, 1001 Broad St, Chattanooga, TN 37402
entries.hours: tool checkout/return via 4th Floor Makerspace during Downtown Library hours
entries.verifiedNote: site carries a board-meeting notice dated July 15, 2026

entries.name: Nashville Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Six categories: musical instruments (guitars, keyboards, ukuleles), tools & electronics (tool kits, radon detectors), kitchen/party gear (cake pans, ice cream makers), fitness equipment, lawn games (bocce, pickleball), and crafts/hobbies (sewing machines, binoculars). 3-week loans, 2 items, holdable system-wide; NPL also lends framed art (Art Lending Library), board games, BCycle passes, and museum passes.
entries.address: Main Library, 615 Church St, Nashville, TN 37219 (items holdable across branches)
entries.hours: branch hours; return at circulation desks only

entries.name: Memphis Public Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: Arts & crafts, cookware, gardening gear (recently expanded via a Clean Memphis partnership), musical instruments, recreation items, and tools & electronics. 7-day loans, 2 items, 18+, waiver at checkout; first-come, first-served.
entries.address: Benjamin L. Hooks Central Library, 3030 Poplar Ave, Memphis, TN 38111 (also Frayser, South, and Whitehaven branches)
entries.hours: branch hours; checkout/return at circulation desks
entries.verifiedNote: page last updated June 12, 2026

entries.name: Nashville Community Tool Share Program (Metro Beautification / NDOT)
entries.description: Free loans of garden and cleanup tools — wheelbarrows, shovels, broadforks, post hole diggers, weed wrenches, loppers — to community groups doing food-garden, cleanup, or beautification projects. Partnership of Metro Beautification, Second Harvest Food Bank, and Tractor Supply; reserve by waiver/reservation form.
entries.address: NDOT Warehouse, 750 S. 5th St, Nashville, TN 37206
entries.verifiedNote: page last updated Nov 3, 2025, live today

entries.name: Memphis City Beautiful — Tool Bank
entries.description: The nation's oldest city beautification commission lends rakes, shovels, brooms, saws, post hole diggers, and hand gardening tools free to groups running neighborhood cleanups and community gardens; request form two weeks ahead.

entries.name: Signal Centers — Assistive Technology Lending Library
entries.description: Nonprofit (a Tennessee Technology Access Program center) with a device loan and lending library: a large donated collection of assistive devices, adaptive equipment, and enabling tech loaned to the public, plus demos and device reuse (186 reutilized devices loaned in a recent year).
entries.hours: not published online; contact for appointments

entries.name: The STAR Center — Assistive Device Loan & Reuse Program
entries.description: West Tennessee disability nonprofit offering free device demonstrations plus "a reuse and loan program for devices and equipment" — borrow-before-you-buy for assistive tech, adapted equipment, and mobility aids.

entries.name: Knox County Public Library — Library of Things (Storytimes To Go kits)
entries.description: Knoxville's only branded "Library of Things" is currently limited to themed Storytimes To Go kits — curated books, music, and educational items for preschool caregivers (topics like oceans, farms, colors). Included for Knoxville coverage; it is not a general tool/gear collection.
entries.address: Lawson McGhee Library, 500 W. Church Ave, Knoxville, TN 37902 (system-wide)

entries.name: Houston ToolBank
entries.type: standalone tool library (nonprofit tool bank — lends to charitable orgs, not individuals)
entries.description: Warehouse-scale nonprofit lending 300+ types of tools and equipment (hand/power tools, generators, event tables/tents, dehumidifiers) to nonprofits and tax-exempt groups across Southeast Texas for pennies on the dollar, plus seven 24/7 "Neighborhood Tool Hubs" and free disaster-recovery tool lending.

entries.name: Toybrary Austin
entries.description: Long-running toy library where member families check out toys (and even bikes) and swap them back when kids get bored; also runs drop-off childcare, Stay & Play, and birthday parties. Events calendar showed activity through July 2026.
entries.hours: seasonal; summer Mon 9am–1pm listed (call for current)

entries.name: Harris County Public Library Tool Library
entries.description: Lends gardening tools — wheelbarrows, shovels, loppers, pruners, hoes, gloves — free to any HCPL cardholder for 14 days; up to 40 items at Baldwin Boettcher Branch @ Mercer Park (Humble) and 10 at Evelyn Meador Branch (Seabrook).
entries.address: Evelyn Meador Branch (Seabrook) and Baldwin Boettcher Branch @ Mercer Park (north Harris County)

entries.name: Temple Tool Library
entries.description: Free tool and equipment lending for home repairs and modifications, run by the City of Temple's District Services for residents 18+ with a Temple library card. Currently operating from a temporary pickup site during renovation of its Historic Post Office home.
entries.address: 505 E Calhoun Ave, Temple, TX (temporary location)
entries.hours: pickups Thu 8–10am & 4–6pm; returns Tue 8–10am & 4–6pm; reserve Mon–Wed by phone (254-298-5670)

entries.name: Houston Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: One of the biggest LoT programs in Texas: cookware/bakeware, craft and STEAM discovery kits, board-game boxes, musical instruments, laptops and hotspots, Tonie/Yoto players, plus a seed library; up to 5 items per category for 3 weeks at Central or any neighborhood library. Page last updated July 10, 2026.
entries.address: pickup at HPL Central Library and neighborhood locations

entries.name: Patrick Heath Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Award-winning small-city library with a dedicated LoT catalog spanning tools, kitchen equipment, electronics, science gear, board games, arts & hobbies, and kids' educational toys — a standout for the San Antonio Hill Country area.
entries.address: 451 N Main St, Bldg 100, Boerne, TX 78006

entries.name: Cozby Library and Community Commons — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends a telescope, metal detector, GoPro, electric keyboard, ukulele, binoculars, bocce set, coding robots, a 110-piece home repair tool set, energy meters — even umbrellas; most items 3 weeks, adult cardholders only.
entries.hours: return to front desk during open hours (see site)

entries.name: Wells Branch Community Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Checks out WiFi hotspots, tablets, Kindles, ukuleles, board games, D&D starter kits, kids' Explorer Kits and "Armchair Traveler" kits; mostly 2-week loans for adult cardholders.

entries.name: Bedford Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Browsable LoT catalog with binoculars, record player, home theater projector, power tools, gardening supplies, baby gear, and STEM kits; available to Bedford residents.

entries.name: Benbrook Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Activity kits (action camera, cake decorating, book binding, laser tag, musical instruments, gaming gear), each bundled with how-to materials; 3-week checkout, one kit at a time, open to resident and non-resident cardholders 18+.

entries.name: Denton Public Library — Discovery Kits
entries.description: More than 100 themed hands-on discovery kits — forensics, electrical engineering, and other STEAM topics — housed largely at the North Branch; a kit-focused take on the Library of Things.
entries.address: North Branch Library, 3020 N Locust St, Denton, TX

entries.name: TPWD Tackle Loaner Program
entries.description: Texas Parks & Wildlife lends rods, reels, and tackle boxes free at dozens of state parks and partner sites across Texas — fill out a borrower form, fish, return the gear; under-17s borrow with an adult, no fishing license needed inside state parks.
entries.address: multiple loaner sites statewide (map on site)

entries.name: Grand Prairie Public Library — Library of Things / tool sharing
entries.description: Reported (DFWChild) to lend cotton candy machines, podcast recording equipment, sewing machines, STEM kits, and a tool-sharing program at the Main Library. City website blocks automated verification, so current status could not be confirmed directly.
entries.address: Main Library, 901 Conover Dr, Grand Prairie, TX
entries.verifiedNote: UNVERIFIED (site unreachable to bots; secondary source only)

entries.name: Salt Lake County Library (The County Library) — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: The state's flagship LoT: bird-watching kits, GoPro cameras, Chromebooks, pasta-maker kits, HappyLight therapy lamps and blood-pressure monitors, reservable at any of the system's branches (7–21 day checkouts, adults only, 5-item limit).
entries.address: items at branches countywide (admin: 8030 S 1825 W, West Jordan, UT 84088)
entries.hours: varies by branch; LoT reservations by phone 801-943-4636

entries.name: Provo City Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Broad LoT including telescopes and a microscope, board games, A/V and home-entertainment equipment, binoculars, a bike repair kit, Chromebooks, hotspots, blood-pressure and radon monitor kits, plus a separate Children's Library of Things and seed library.

entries.name: Park City Library — Unique Items
entries.description: Description: "Unique Items" collection spanning board games, sustainability, entertainment, lifestyle, outdoor recreation and technology categories; cardholders check items out, and visitors without a card can use items in-library for up to 4 hours.

entries.name: Summit County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Three-branch county system lending Roku streaming sticks, mobile hotspots, disc players, a projector, a beginner telescope, an induction cooktop and an "Energy Detective" home-energy audit kit.
entries.address: 1885 W Ute Blvd, Park City, UT 84098 (Kimball Junction Branch; also Coalville and Kamas)

entries.name: Davis County Library — Equipment Checkout
entries.description: Description: County system with a dedicated "Equipment Checkout" collection surfaced as its own material type in the library catalog; a modest LoT rather than a branded program.
entries.address: not published (branches countywide; headquarters in Farmington)

entries.name: Utah Assistive Technology Program (UATP) — AT Lending Library
entries.city: Logan / Salt Lake City / Uintah Basin / southern Utah
entries.type: other (assistive-technology lending library, Utah State University program)
entries.description: Description: Works "just like a public library, but instead of books you check out assistive technology" — knee scooters, manual and power wheelchairs, motorized scooters and daily-living aids, mostly free to borrow, with inventory searchable via the AT4ALL database.
entries.address: labs at USU Logan, Salt Lake City, the Uintah Basin and southern Utah (street addresses via site)
entries.hours: demo days first Tuesday of each month 10am–2pm; otherwise by appointment (1-800-524-5152)

entries.name: Fletcher Free Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Burlington's city library lends board games, cooking equipment, arts & crafts gear, garden tools, sports equipment, and tech (including a telescope) from its Main and New North End locations.
entries.address: 235 College St, Burlington, VT 05401 (also 1127 North Ave branch)

entries.name: South Burlington Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends games, consumer electronics, and tools with a library card; the collection lives on the library's second floor and accepts community donations.

entries.name: Brownell Library — Library of Things
entries.description: An "ever expanding" collection spanning knitting kits, sewing supplies, disc golf and pickleball sets, projectors, karaoke machines, telescopes, metal detectors, STEM kits, and Chromebooks.

entries.name: Ilsley Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Lends tools, puzzles, games, laptops, outdoor gear — and, distinctively, Cannondale e-bikes borrowed with a library card and picked up at local shop Frog Hollow Bikes (May–Oct).

entries.name: Brooks Memorial Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Year-round kitchen tools plus seasonal garden tools and e-bikes; also hiking poles and wifi hotspots, reservable through the library's LibCal system.

entries.name: Norman Williams Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: A small but growing collection of tools, electronic equipment, and games; borrowers sign a lending agreement and keep items for two weeks.

entries.name: Come Alive Outside Gear Library
entries.description: Lends tents, sleeping bags, fishing equipment, lanterns, outdoor games, Adirondack chairs, kayaks, and winter gear; free for Rutland Free Library cardholders, with low rates otherwise. Launched with a Vermont Outdoor Recreation (VOREC) grant in partnership with Rutland Free Library; covered by local press in June 2026.

entries.name: Cville Tool Library
entries.description: Volunteer-run nonprofit lending library with 1,000+ tools — hand and power tools, garden gear, plus oddities like a pressure canner, carpet cleaner, and walnut cracker. Sliding-scale annual membership; grew out of Virginia Organizing and opened in 2022.
entries.address: 1740 Broadway St. #13 (Visible Records building), Charlottesville, VA 22902

entries.name: Richmond ToolBank
entries.description: Lends a $850,000+ inventory of tools and equipment to nonprofits, schools, and volunteer groups (not individuals) for service projects — 18,800+ tools borrowed in 2025. Membership is free; agencies pay ~6% of retail value per week borrowed.

entries.name: Arlington Public Library — Library of Things & The Shed
entries.description: One of the region's most developed public-library collections: DIY tools, gadgets, board games, hotspots, American Girl dolls, memory kits, and nature backpacks, plus "The Shed," a dedicated garden-tool lending shed.

entries.name: Fairfax County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Large county-wide collection: thermal cameras, radon and air-quality monitors, Chromebooks with hotspots, binoculars, board games, STEAM and history kits, framed artwork, and nature backpacks with Virginia State Parks day passes.

entries.name: Virginia Beach Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Broad collection across branches including sewing machines, Cricut presses, telescopes, GoPros, coding robots, board games, American Girl dolls, accessibility kits — and, fittingly for the beach, surfboards. Three LoT items per card at a time.

entries.name: Jefferson-Madison Regional Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Five-county system lending craft kits, ukuleles, yoga and light-therapy gear, Wi-Fi hotspots, robots, a 40-item toy library, memory kits for seniors, museum passes, and a seed library; most items borrowable at any branch for three weeks.

entries.name: Roanoke County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Eight-category collection (Accessibility, Outdoor, Play, STEM, Tech and more) including HappyLight therapy lamps, magnifiers, and Reader Pens that read printed text aloud.

entries.name: Alexandria Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Smaller "Library of Things" menu focused on practical tech and kids' items: mobile hotspots, Chromebooks, thermal cameras, Playaway Launchpads/Bookpacks, and American Girl dolls, borrowable across four branches.

entries.name: Phinney Tool Lending Library (Phinney Neighborhood Association)
entries.description: Founded 1977, one of the oldest tool libraries in the US. 2,000+ tools from staple guns to torque wrenches, part of the PNA "DIY Hub" with a woodshop and repair collective; requires PNA membership, small per-week tool fees.

entries.name: West Seattle Tool Library
entries.description: 3,300+ home-maintenance, garden, and automotive tools on suggested-donation memberships ($7–$250); also runs Fix-It Nights, open workshop hours, and classes.

entries.name: Capitol Hill Tool Library (Sustainable Capitol Hill)
entries.type: standalone tool library / nonprofit Library of Things
entries.description: All-volunteer, 3,000+ items well beyond tools: kitchen appliances, sewing machines, garden gear, and outdoor equipment, plus an on-site member workshop. Free or pay-what-you-can membership.

entries.name: Ballard Tool Library (Sustainable Ballard)
entries.description: Neighborhood tool library operating since 2018, with walk-in hours and appointment pickup through a MyTurn inventory.

entries.name: NE Seattle Tool Library (Seattle REconomy)
entries.description: One of two Seattle REconomy tool libraries (one membership covers both); tools plus a woodshop, classes, bike repair help, and a secondhand building-materials store under the same nonprofit.

entries.name: Shoreline Tool Library (Seattle REconomy)
entries.description: Seattle REconomy's second, larger-hours location; tools plus sewing machines, pressure washers, even board games, with frequent classes ("Tool School for Women+", sewing).

entries.name: SE Seattle Tool Library
entries.description: Volunteer-run tool lending plus training and workshop space for south-end neighbors; entrance is in the alley off MLK Jr. Way.
entries.address: 4425 MLK Jr. Way S, Seattle, WA 98108

entries.name: South King Tool Library
entries.description: Two-location tool library with 33,000+ checkouts logged, a community garden, repair events, and workshops; opened a new Auburn branch.
entries.address: 1700 S 340th Pl, Federal Way, WA 98003 and 410 H St NE, Auburn, WA 98002
entries.hours: Federal Way Mon 10a–2p, Wed 4–6p, Sat 10a–2p; Auburn Thu 4–7p

entries.name: Tacoma Tool Library
entries.description: 2,500+ tools (power, hand, yard, sewing) lent free for two weeks; hosts weekly Fix-it Night and sewing programs. Located in the downtown Main Library building.

entries.name: Vashon Tool Library
entries.description: Island community tool library with online reservations and periodic tool sales; suggested $40 donation to join.
entries.address: 18870 103rd Ave SW (O2 building, off SW 188th St), Vashon, WA 98070

entries.name: The Mountaineers Gear Library
entries.description: Outdoor gear lending (tents, snowshoes, camping kit) since 2018, aimed at youth programs, families, and community organizations as well as individuals; flat-fee 1- or 6-month memberships with tiered pricing. 7700 Sand Point Way NE, Seattle, WA 98115 (Seattle Program Center)

entries.name: Spokane Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Borrowable "things" collection alongside book-club kits, museum/culture passes, and The Hive maker studios; checkout via library card at city branches. 906 W Main Ave, Spokane, WA 99201 (Central; multiple branches)

entries.name: Fort Vancouver Regional Libraries — Library of Things
entries.description: Telescopes, drill kits, ukuleles, kids' floor puzzles and more at participating branches; 2 items at a time for resident cardholders. District-wide (Vancouver-area branches)

entries.name: JeffCo Community Tool Library (Port Townsend Marine Science Center)
entries.description: Community tool lending run as part of the science center's "JeffCo Repair" waste-reduction initiative, alongside repair events. Port Townsend, WA (see site)

entries.name: Morgantown Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Newer collection (launched 2026) lending birding backpacks with binoculars, citizen-science kits (water quality, biodiversity, snowflakes), bocce and cornhole sets, board and video games, a children's guitar and amp, Yoto players, and a blood pressure monitor.

entries.name: South Charleston Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: The state's most colorful collection: metal detectors, fishing poles, ghost-hunting kits, vinyl records, Roku streamers, VHS-to-digital converters, therapy lights, board and video games, and seeds.

entries.name: Kanawha County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Modest but active nontraditional lending: T-Mobile Wi-Fi hotspots (unlimited data, up to 15 devices) available at branches, plus BBC micro:bit pocket computers for learning to code.

entries.name: Milwaukee Tool Library
entries.description: Free-to-join tool library lending a broad tool/equipment collection, sustained by its affiliated Shaky Hands Union print lab; enrollment online then in person with ID.
entries.hours: summer — M & W 9–4:30, F 9–12 (July 2026 schedule posted)

entries.name: Madison Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: System-wide specialty lending: BCycle e-bike passes, birding backpacks, disc golf kits, adaptive toys, light-therapy lamps, record players & vinyl, energy meters, graphing calculators, seeds; page updated May 2025.
entries.address: 9 locations (Central: 201 W. Mifflin St, Madison, WI 53703)

entries.name: Madison Tool Library
entries.description: Low-cost tool and equipment rentals for Madison residents — woodworking, plumbing, welding, construction, lawn & garden, automotive; $20/mo or $100/yr membership plus small per-tool fees, pickups arranged by email.
entries.verifiedNote: self-described as "slowly getting underway"

entries.name: Low Tech Tool Library (Low Technology Institute)
entries.description: Rural nonprofit tool library ("saws, tile cutters, and hammers instead of Hemingway") lending hand, power, garden, plumbing, bike, and auto tools; most loans free for a week, small deposits/fees on some power tools.

entries.name: Racine Public Library — Beyond Books
entries.description: Instruments (ukuleles, steel tongue drum, keyboards), Cricut heat press, sewing machine, looms, cameras and green-screen kits, game consoles, laptops, plus tool-library items and a seed library; ~1-week loans, no late fees.

entries.name: Mead Public Library — "Things" collection
entries.description: Lends karaoke machines, electronic drum kits, Arduino boards, vacuum sealers, cake pans, STEM and birdwatching kits, museum/attraction passes, and ~200 book club kits.

entries.name: UW-Madison College Library Tool Library
entries.description: Campus tool library on College Library's 1st floor: power drills, sewing machines, soldering irons, voltmeters, tool kits; 7-day student loans plus an at-cost vending machine for nails, screws, and thread.
entries.address: 600 N. Park St (Helen C. White Hall), Madison, WI 53706

entries.name: Atwood Tool Library (Sustainable Atwood)
entries.description: Neighborhood tool library at Zion Lutheran Church lending home-repair, bike, garden, carpentry, and plumbing tools; $20/yr membership. Founded 2014.
entries.verifiedNote: (parent org website down; current status unclear)

entries.name: Teton County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Browsable Library of Things spanning crafts and hobbies, electronics, health items, household tools, and outdoor recreation gear, at both the Jackson and Alta branches; also lends Wi-Fi hotspots and activity passes.
entries.address: 125 Virginian Ln., Jackson, WY 83001 (Alta branch: 50 Alta School Rd.)

entries.name: Natrona County Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: Lends astronomy binoculars, car jump-starters, ukuleles, gardening kits, video games, Chromebooks, "WiFi2Go" hotspots, and Wyoming State Park passes through its Check-out State Parks program; also runs a seed library and sports "Library Gear."

entries.name: Campbell County Public Library — Library of Things
entries.description: Description: One of Wyoming's broadest collections: Cricut Maker and heat press, sewing machine, telescope, thermal imager, radon detectors, GoPro and content-creator kits, canning kit, PA system, and State Parks backpacks; local press has reported rising checkouts.
entries.address: 2101 S. 4J Rd., Gillette, WY 82718 (branch in Wright)

entries.name: Crook County Library System — "WOW! @ your library"
entries.description: Description: Small-town Library of Things ("Weird, Odd & Wonderful") with 3-week loans of kitchen and cake-pan kits, blood-pressure and radon kits, State Parks backpacks, a Yoto player, and "Library 2 Business" podcast/photo gear (ring light, webcam, light box).
entries.address: street address not published (PO Box 910, Sundance, WY 82729)

entries.name: Women in the Tetons Gear Closet
entries.description: Description: All-volunteer, all-female nonprofit lending donated outdoor gear (ski boots, jackets, helmets, camping and climbing gear) free to women, coordinated out of board members' homes via email/Instagram. Soft-launched fall 2025 with covered by KHOL in Feb 2026; full official launch expected with the 2026 scholarship season.
entries.verifiedNote: site live and lending on request, though the org labels the program not yet officially launched

entries.name: Wyoming Wildlife Federation Gear Library
entries.type: other (free hunting gear and game-processing equipment library)
entries.description: Description: Free checkout of hunting gear (packs, binoculars, harnesses, technical clothing, including women's sizes) and wild-game butchering equipment (grinders, dehydrators, vacuum sealers) for Lander-area residents; launched with a Dec 2024 LOR Foundation grant and covered by the Lander Journal in Oct 2025.
entries.address: not published (WWF is headquartered in Lander)
entries.verifiedNote: (org website loads as of July 2026 but has no dedicated gear-library page; program's existence confirmed only via Oct 2025 news coverage and July 2025 social posts)
